## PAGE 04070: (2026x) Summary and Overview

- page_id: `272736546`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736546/2026x+Summary+and+Overview

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

923507957

923507959

923507958

**Description**

The Summary & Overview viewpoint is for modeling the executive-level summary information in a consistent form. It provides executive-level summary information in a consistent form that allows quick reference and comparison between architectural descriptions.

The Summary and Overview Measurements (Pm-Me) domain depicts the measurements. The intended usage of The Summary and Overview Measurements (Pm-Me) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

The Summary & Overview domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Summary and Overview diagram' space=''] . It shows a quick overview of an architecture description and a summary of the analysis. In the initial phases of architecture development, it serves as a planning guide. Upon completion of architecture, it provides a summary of findings and any conducted analysis.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Date and Time Summary Table' space=''] depicts all dates and times specified in the ISO8601 date-time format.
- Reports (.docx): - Summary and Overview. - All View.

The Summary and Overview Measurements (Pm-Me) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Typical Measurements table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Actual Measurements table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='']
- Actual Measurements (.xlsx)

****

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731596 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04071: (2026x) Supported and unsupported SysML v1 concepts

- page_id: `272731596`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731596/2026x+Supported+and+unsupported+SysML+v1+concepts
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:41:55.810+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x) Modelica export
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### SysML v1 supported concepts

The translator can be used for both high-level black-box architecture export, when simulation engineer have to implement all block behaviors in Modelica tool, or full or partial behavior implementation too.

Export engine takes one block as a starting point and exports all containing model structure recursively including:

- [Block](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Block)and [interface](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Interface+Block) definitions.
- [Parts](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Part+Property), [value properties](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Value+Property), [flow properties](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Flow+Property) with their default/[initial values](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Initial+Values) and associated [units](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Unit). We highly recommend to use part names, otherwise the artificial names are exported.
- [Proxy](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Proxy+Port)and [flow ports](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Flow+Port) and [connectors](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Connectors) among them.
- Parametrics – [constraints blocks](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Constraint+Block), [constraint properties](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Constraint+Property), [constraint parameters](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Constraint+Parameter) and connections among them
- [State Machines](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) State+Machines)
- [IBD](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) SysML+Internal+Block+Diagram)diagrams

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729366 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04072: (2026x) Supported CSS

- page_id: `272729366`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729366/2026x+Supported+CSS
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:34:55.741+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Appendix D: HTML tag support
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### Background

The shorthand property for the background property is 'background', which is used for setting an individual background style. Such an attribute can be used in *RTF, ODT,* and *DOCX* report templates.

##### Color Specification

Color attributes such as background-color can be specified by either a keyword or a numerical RGB specification (hexadecimal, integer, and percentage value). The list of Color Keywords and Corresponding RGB Specification is displayed in the table below.

| Color | RGB SpecificationHexadecimal Code | RGB Specification Integer | RGB Specification Percentage |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Maroon | #800000 | RGB(128,0,0) | RGB(50%,0%,0%) |
| Red | #FF0000 | RGB(255,0,0) | RGB(100%,0%,0%) |
| Orange | #FFA500 | RGB(255,165,0) | RGB(100%,65%,0%) |
| Yellow | #FFFF00 | RGB(255,255,0) | RGB(100%,100%,0%) |
| Olive | #808000 | RGB(128,128,0) | RGB(50%,50%,0%) |
| Purple | #800080 | RGB(128,0,128) | RGB(50%,0%,50%) |
| Fuchsia | #FF00FF | RGB(255,0,255) | RGB(100%,0%,100%) |
| White | #FFFFFF | RGB(255,255,255) | RGB(100%,100%,100%) |
| Lime | #00FF00 | RGB(0,255,0) | RGB(0%,100%,0%) |
| Green | #008000 | RGB(0,128,0) | RGB(0%,50%,0%) |
| Navy | #000080 | RGB(0,0,128) | RGB(0%,0%,50%) |
| Blue | #0000FF | RGB(0,0,255) | RGB(0%,0%,100%) |
| Aqua | #00FFFF | RGB(0,255,255) | RGB(0%,100%,100%) |
| Teal | #008080 | RGB(0,128,128) | RGB(0%,50%,50%) |
| Black | #000000 | RGB(0,0,0) | RGB(0%,0%,0%) |
| Silver | #C0C0C0 | RGB(192,192,192) | RGB(75%,75%,75%) |
| Gray | #808080 | RGB(128,128,128) | RGB(50%,50%,50%) |

The format of an RGB specification value in form of the hexadecimal notation is a '#' immediately followed by either three or six hexadecimal characters. The three-digit RGB notation (#rgb) is converted to the six-digit form (#rrggbb) by replicating digits. For example, #FB0 is converted to #FFBB00.

For example:

<p style = “background-color:#FB0”> 
<p style = “background-color:#FFBB00”>

The RGB specification Integer and Percentage form is "rgb" followed by RGB values in the same format in brackets. You cannot use both integer and percentage at the same time. In the case that an RGB value is more than a maximum value (255 or 100%) or less than a minimum (0 or 0%), the value will be set to the maximum or minimum value instead, for example:

<p style = "background-color:rgb(-100,500,0)">

<p style = "background-color:rgb(0,255,0)">

<p style = "background-colr:rgb(-100%,200%,0%)">

<p style = "background-colr:rgb(0%,100%,0%)">

The color used in all of the above examples is lime.

##### Supported Tags

The ‘background-color’ property can be used in the following tags:

```text
b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.
```

For example:

#### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Border

The shorthand property for the border property is 'border', which is used for setting the same width, color, and style of all four borders on four sides of a box. Border can be used in *RTF, ODT*, and *DOCX* report templates.

##### Border Width

Border width properties specify the width of border areas. The value of the border property is length. The border width properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:

- 'border-top-width'
- 'border-right-width'
- 'border-bottom-width'
- 'border-left-width'

##### Length Specification

The format of a length value is a real number immediately followed by a unit identifier. There are two types of length units: relative and absolute. Relative length units specify a length relative to another length property of the displaying device.

*Relative units* are:

- 
  - px pixels, relative to the displaying device.

*Absolute units* are:

- 
  - in inches, 1 inch is equal to 2.54 centimeters.
  - cm centimeters.
  - mm millimeters.
  - pt points - the points used by CSS 2.1 are equal to 1/72nd of an inch.
  - pc picas - 1 pica is equal to 12 points.

##### Border Color

Border color properties specify the border colors of a box. The value of the border color has two meanings:

- Color Specifies a border color. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom renderers sample' space='DEVG'] for more details on color.
- Transparent Referred to as a transparent border.

The border color properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:

- 'border-top-color'
- 'border-right-color'
- 'border-bottom-color'
- 'border-left-color'

##### Border Style

The Border style properties specify the style of a box's border lines. The border style may take one of the following 10 values:

1. none This value will create no border because the computed border width is zero.
2. hidden The same as 'none'.
3. dotted This value will create a dotted border.
4. dashed This value will create a dashed border.
5. solid This value will create a single line segment border.
6. double This value will create a two solid line border.
7. groove This value will create a border that looks as though it were carved into the rendering surface.
8. ridge The opposite of 'groove'. This value will create a border that looks as though it were coming out of the rendering surface.
9. inset This value will create a border that looks as though it were embedded in the rendering surface.
10. outset The opposite of 'inset'. This value will create a border that looks as though it were coming out of the rendering surface.

- The supported border values for ODF documents are none , hidden , solid , and double .
- The supported border values for RTF documents are none , hidden , dotted , dashed , solid , and double .
- The supported border values for DOCX documents are none , hidden , dotted , dashed , solid , double , outset , and inset ( groove and ridge will be rendered as solid).

The border style properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:

- 'border-top-style'
- 'border-right-style'
- 'border-bottom-style'
- 'border-left-style'

##### Supported Tags

Border properties can be used in the following tags:

```text
h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, td, th, p, and div.
```

For example, if you want to have a red border, type the following line of code, see the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Margin

Margin properties specify the width of margin area within a box. The 'margin' property is a shorthand property for setting the margin of all four sides while the other margin properties only set their respective side. The value of the margin property is length (See for more details). Margin can be used in *RTF, ODT, ODP, DOCX* and *PPTX* report templates.

The margin properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:

- 'margin-top'
- 'margin-right'
- 'margin-bottom'
- 'margin-left'

##### Supported Tags

The supported tags for margins are as follows:

```text
h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, table (supported only in RTF, ODT, and DOCX), p, and div.
```

For example, if you want to set a specific margin width for your cells, type the lines of code as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Padding

Padding properties specify the width of padding area within a box. The 'padding' property is a shorthand property for setting the padding for all four sides while the other padding properties only set their respective side. The value of the padding property is length (See for more details) adding can be used in *RTF, ODT* and *DOCX* report templates.

The padding properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:

- 'padding-top'
- 'padding-right'
- 'padding-bottom'
- 'padding-left'

##### Supported Tags

The supported tags for padding are as follows:

```text
h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, td, th, p, and div.
```

- The supported border values of table tags for RTF documents are margin-left and margin-right.for more details.
- The supported border values of table tags for DOCX documents are margin-left and margin-right.
- The supported border values of all tags for PPTX documents are margin-left, margin-top and margin-bottom.

For example, if you want to create specific padding styles for your cells, type the lines of code shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Color

The 'color' property specifies the foreground color of an element's text contents. Only color values are allowed in this property (See Color Specification for more details on color).

##### Supported Tags

The supported tags for colors are as follows:

```text
b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.
```

For example, if you want to have a green text color, type the lines of code indicated in figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Display

The 'display' property specifies how text will be displayed. The possible display values are:

- block : This value will make an element appear in the output report.
- none : This value will make an element disappear from the output report.

##### Supported Tags

The supported tags for the display property are:

```text
br, b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li (supported only in RTF, DOCX, and PPTX), table, tr, p, div, span, sup, and sub.
```

For example, if you want to display an element but do not want to display the other(s), type the lines of code as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as indicated below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Font

The 'font' property is a shorthand property for setting, except the following:

- 'font family'
- 'font-style'
- 'font-variant'
- 'font-weight'
- 'font-size'

##### Font Family

Specify a prioritized list of font family names and/or generic family names.

##### Font Style

Specify either normal or italic face (within the specified font family).

##### Font Variant

Specify either normal or small-caps of variant (within the specified font family). Font variant is not supported in *XLSX* report templates.

##### Font Weight

Specifies the weight of the font. The following values are defined:

- normal , lighter , 100 , 200 , 300 , and 400 will be rendered as ‘ normal ’.
- bold , bolder , 500 , 600 , 700 , 800 , and 900 will be rendered as ‘ bold ’.

##### Font size

Specify the font size. Possible values include xx-small, x-small, small, medium, large, xx-large, and the integer number from 1 to 7.

##### Supported Tags

The supported tags for the font property are as follows:

```text
b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.
```

For example, if you want to have bold fonts in small caps, type the lines of code as displayed in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Text Align

The 'text align' property describes how inline content of a block is aligned. Possible values of the text align property include: **left**, **right**, **center** and **justify**. The ‘text align’ property can be used in *RTF, ODT, ODP, DOCX,* and *PPTX* report templates.

##### Supported Tags

The supported tags for the text align property are as follows:

```text
table, tr, td, th, p, and div.
```

For example, if you want to align your paragraph to the left, center, and right, type the lines of code as shown in the Figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as displayed in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Text Transform

The 'text transform' property controls capitalization effects of an element's text. Possible values of the 'text transform' property include:

1. capitalize This will write the first character in an uppercase letter. Other characters will not be affected.
2. uppercase This will write each word in upper case letters.
3. lowercase This will write each word in lower case letters.
4. none This will not create any capitalization effects.

##### Supported Tags

The supported tags for the text transform property are as follows:

```text
b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.
```

For example, if you want to make all letters in a paragraph become uppercases, type the line of code displayed in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### White-Space

The 'white-space' property affects the vertical position of white-space inside a lined box. White-space can be used in *RTF, ODT, DOCX* and *PPTX* report templates. Possible values of the 'white-space' property include:

1. normal To collapse sequences of white-space and break lines as necessary to fill line boxes.
2. pre To prevent from collapsing sequences of white-space. Lines are only broken at newlines in the source or at occurrences of "\A" in the generated content. Report Wizard will render this value as the <pre> tag.
3. nowrap To collapse white-space as for 'normal' but suppressing line breaks within text.
4. pre-wrap To prevent user agents from collapsing sequences of white-space. Lines are broken at newlines in the source, at occurrences of "\A" in the generated content, and as necessary to fill line boxes. This value will not be rendered as pre.
5. pre-line To direct user agents to collapse sequences of white-space. Lines are broken at newlines in the source, at occurrences of "\A" in the generated content, and as necessary to fill line boxes. This value will not be rendered as pre.

##### Supported Tags

The supported tags for the 'white-space' property are as follows:

```text
b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.
```

For example, if you want to preserve some white-space, type these lines of code as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Width

The 'width' property specifies the contents width of each box generated by a block-level. The value of the width property is length (See for more details). Width can be used in *RTF, ODT,* and *DOCX* report templates.

The supported tag for the width property includes table.

For example, if you want to have a specific table width, type the lines of code as described in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Text Decoration

The 'text decoration' property describes decorations that are added to text. The possible values of the text decoration property are as follows:

- none To produce no text decoration.
- underline To underline each line of text.
- overline To add a line above each line of text.
- line-through To add a line that strikes through each line of text.
- blink To add the blinking effect to text (being visible and invisible alternatively). The blink text decoration supports only the ODT report template.

Report Wizard does not currently support the overline text decoration.

##### Supported Tags

The supported tags for the text decoration property are:

```text
b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.
```

For example, if you want to create a line-through text, type the line of code as displayed in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Vertical Align

The 'vertical align' property affects the vertical position of an element inside a lined box. This property can be used in *RTF, ODT* and *DOCX* report templates. Possible values of the vertical align property include:

- baseline This value will align the baseline of a box with the baseline of the parent box. Leave the vertical alignment to be default when you encounter this value.
- middle This value will align the vertical midpoint of a box.
- top This value will align the top of the aligned sub-tree with the top of a line box.
- bottom This value will align the bottom of the aligned sub-tree with the bottom of a line box.

The supported tags for the vertical align property are: table, tr, td, and th, for example, see figure below:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h2>Background</h2><p>The shorthand property for the background property is 'background', which is used for setting an individual background style. Such an attribute can be used in <em>RTF, ODT,</em> and <em>DOCX</em> report templates.</p><h3>Color Specification</h3><p>Color attributes such as background-color can be specified by either a keyword or a numerical RGB specification (hexadecimal, integer, and percentage value). The list of Color Keywords and Corresponding RGB Specification is displayed in the table below.</p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Color </th><th><p>RGB <br />Specification<br />Hexadecimal <br />Code </p></th><th>RGB <br />Specification <br />Integer </th><th>RGB <br />Specification <br />Percentage </th></tr><tr class=""><td>Maroon </td><td>#800000</td><td>RGB(128,0,0) </td><td>RGB(50%,0%,0%) </td></tr><tr class=""><td>Red </td><td>#FF0000</td><td>RGB(255,0,0)</td><td>RGB(100%,0%,0%) </td></tr><tr class=""><td>Orange</td><td>#FFA500</td><td>RGB(255,165,0) </td><td>RGB(100%,65%,0%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Yellow</td><td colspan="1">#FFFF00</td><td colspan="1">RGB(255,255,0)</td><td colspan="1">RGB(100%,100%,0%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Olive</td><td colspan="1">#808000</td><td colspan="1">RGB(128,128,0)</td><td colspan="1">RGB(50%,50%,0%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Purple</td><td colspan="1">#800080</td><td colspan="1">RGB(128,0,128)</td><td colspan="1">RGB(50%,0%,50%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Fuchsia</td><td colspan="1">#FF00FF</td><td colspan="1">RGB(255,0,255)</td><td colspan="1">RGB(100%,0%,100%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">White</td><td colspan="1">#FFFFFF</td><td colspan="1">RGB(255,255,255)</td><td colspan="1">RGB(100%,100%,100%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Lime</td><td colspan="1">#00FF00</td><td colspan="1">RGB(0,255,0)</td><td colspan="1">RGB(0%,100%,0%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Green</td><td colspan="1">#008000</td><td colspan="1">RGB(0,128,0)</td><td colspan="1">RGB(0%,50%,0%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Navy</td><td colspan="1">#000080</td><td colspan="1">RGB(0,0,128) </td><td colspan="1">RGB(0%,0%,50%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Blue</td><td colspan="1">#0000FF</td><td colspan="1">RGB(0,0,255)</td><td colspan="1">RGB(0%,0%,100%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Aqua</td><td colspan="1">#00FFFF</td><td colspan="1">RGB(0,255,255)</td><td colspan="1">RGB(0%,100%,100%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Teal</td><td colspan="1">#008080</td><td colspan="1">RGB(0,128,128)</td><td colspan="1">RGB(0%,50%,50%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Black</td><td colspan="1"> #000000 </td><td colspan="1">RGB(0,0,0) </td><td colspan="1">RGB(0%,0%,0%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Silver</td><td colspan="1">#C0C0C0</td><td colspan="1">RGB(192,192,192)</td><td colspan="1">RGB(75%,75%,75%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Gray</td><td colspan="1">#808080</td><td colspan="1">RGB(128,128,128)</td><td colspan="1">RGB(50%,50%,50%)</td></tr></tbody></table><p>The format of an RGB specification value in form of the hexadecimal notation is a '#' immediately followed by either three or six hexadecimal characters. The three-digit RGB notation (#rgb) is converted to the six-digit form (#rrggbb) by replicating digits. For example, #FB0 is converted to #FFBB00.</p><p>For example:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">&lt;p style = “background-color:#FB0”&gt;<br />&lt;p style = “background-color:#FFBB00”&gt;</p><p>The RGB specification Integer and Percentage form is &quot;rgb&quot; followed by RGB values in the same format in brackets. You cannot use both integer and percentage at the same time. In the case that an RGB value is more than a maximum value (255 or 100%) or less than a minimum (0 or 0%), the value will be set to the maximum or minimum value instead, for example:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">&lt;p style = &quot;background-color:rgb(-100,500,0)&quot;&gt;</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">&lt;p style = &quot;background-color:rgb(0,255,0)&quot;&gt;</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">&lt;p style = &quot;background-colr:rgb(-100%,200%,0%)&quot;&gt;</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">&lt;p style = &quot;background-colr:rgb(0%,100%,0%)&quot;&gt;</p><p>The color used in all of the above examples is lime.</p><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The ‘background-color’ property can be used in the following tags:</p><pre>b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub. </pre><p>For example:</p><h2><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_1.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_2.png" /></ac:image></h2><h2><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Border</span></h2><p>The shorthand property for the border property is 'border', which is used for setting the same width, color, and style of all four borders on four sides of a box. Border can be used in <em>RTF, ODT</em>, and <em>DOCX</em> report templates.</p><h3>Border Width</h3><p>Border width properties specify the width of border areas. The value of the border property is length. The border width properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:</p><ul><li data-uuid="9ab48542-2554-49b7-9a6b-c716b650296e"><pre>'border-top-width'</pre></li><li data-uuid="feaf09b1-ffbc-4db2-a414-369011f97d27"><pre>'border-right-width'</pre></li><li data-uuid="0fcb49f0-4391-4892-814b-5cfcf9d5cd65"><pre>'border-bottom-width'</pre></li><li data-uuid="efecbdb0-58ca-4631-9aa3-fb5d3a0b00c1"><pre>'border-left-width'</pre></li></ul><h3>Length Specification</h3><p>The format of a length value is a real number immediately followed by a unit identifier. There are two types of length units: relative and absolute. Relative length units specify a length relative to another length property of the displaying device.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>Relative units</em> are:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;" data-uuid="7fc4ccae-adde-4b54-a0d6-68e1907822cf"><ul><li><strong>px</strong> <br />pixels, relative to the displaying device.</li></ul></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>Absolute units</em> are:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;" data-uuid="1e4edb9d-29e9-4945-95a4-ce0bbb25cfc7"><ul><li><strong>in</strong> <br />inches, 1 inch is equal to 2.54 centimeters.</li><li><strong>cm<br /></strong>centimeters.</li><li><strong>mm<br /></strong>millimeters.</li><li><strong>pt<br /></strong>points - the points used by CSS 2.1 are equal to 1/72nd of an inch.</li><li><strong>pc<br /></strong>picas - 1 pica is equal to 12 points.</li></ul></li></ul><h3>Border Color</h3><p>Border color properties specify the border colors of a box. The value of the border color has two meanings:</p><ul><li data-uuid="6ee37b8f-d951-4304-96ad-b327248e4667"><strong>Color<br /></strong>Specifies a border color. See <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG" ri:content-title="Custom renderers sample" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Color Specification]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for more details on color.</li><li data-uuid="4d0e65c1-994a-47ca-9985-920e2ec1f33c"><strong>Transparent<br /></strong>Referred to as a transparent border.</li></ul><p>The border color properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:</p><ul><li data-uuid="a2ba11e3-de6d-42a3-ae6d-21eb456455bd"><pre>'border-top-color'</pre></li><li data-uuid="a430357b-0493-416e-9042-d7b0a2b8febb"><pre>'border-right-color'</pre></li><li data-uuid="711f130a-c4b7-4f73-ad47-13826bedfee7"><pre>'border-bottom-color'</pre></li><li data-uuid="a30ddadf-e9e6-48e0-b87b-578b0ed6ae7e"><pre>'border-left-color'</pre></li></ul><h3>Border Style</h3><p>The Border style properties specify the style of a box's border lines. The border style may take one of the following 10 values:</p><ol><li data-uuid="78214c4a-dc69-48e6-a5b6-bfb3fe26cea9"><strong>none<br /></strong>This value will create no border because the computed border width is zero.</li><li data-uuid="3f57a534-4b35-427a-9480-efe2dcb4a2e1"><strong>hidden<br /></strong>The same as 'none'.</li><li data-uuid="980bc143-1ba2-4648-8e30-e3e4f93d7d35"><strong>dotted<br /></strong>This value will create a dotted border.</li><li data-uuid="34ff2f1e-8159-40a5-8337-bffe56538cde"><strong>dashed<br /></strong>This value will create a dashed border.</li><li data-uuid="1d197639-d4e3-49bc-a1e2-ccaea374f2e7"><strong>solid<br /></strong>This value will create a single line segment border.</li><li data-uuid="88e3ce73-0686-47b8-acf2-f9497ac4bf4f"><strong>double<br /></strong>This value will create a two solid line border.</li><li data-uuid="412a5153-0da0-4d10-808f-3f18c59bc2da"><strong>groove<br /></strong>This value will create a border that looks as though it were carved into the rendering surface.</li><li data-uuid="364894f8-13df-4348-a1b2-86c46be1b1e6"><strong>ridge<br /></strong>The opposite of 'groove'. This value will create a border that looks as though it were coming out of the rendering surface.</li><li data-uuid="063e6392-2448-4757-b400-b9365de45588"><strong>inset<br /></strong>This value will create a border that looks as though it were embedded in the rendering surface.</li><li data-uuid="1fd2d5a3-6263-4abe-ba06-850a7e9ee841"><strong>outset<br /></strong>The opposite of 'inset'. This value will create a border that looks as though it were coming out of the rendering surface.<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="786398f3-dd2e-487f-91ee-979b67f3ca16"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="5e28891d-f040-467d-950a-9109bb236760">The supported border values for <em>ODF</em> documents are <strong>none</strong>, <strong>hidden</strong>, <strong>solid</strong>, and <strong>double</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="30f256c3-9215-441b-a0c5-db70187a38ca">The supported border values for <em>RTF</em> documents are <strong>none</strong>, <strong>hidden</strong>,<strong>dotted</strong>, <strong>dashed</strong>, <strong>solid</strong>, and <strong>double</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="8e468ff7-86b6-467c-8947-83d2c1832a02">The supported border values for <em>DOCX</em> documents are <strong>none</strong>, <strong>hidden</strong>,<strong>dotted</strong>, <strong>dashed</strong>,<strong> solid</strong>,<strong> double</strong>, <strong>outset</strong>, and <strong>inset</strong> (<strong>groove</strong> and <strong>ridge</strong> will be rendered as solid).</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The border style properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:</p><ul><li data-uuid="73f68d67-6e9c-4866-a0aa-7caff0228c9c"><pre>'border-top-style'</pre></li><li data-uuid="af8bf20b-f86b-499f-a0b8-5a1a4a565a98"><pre>'border-right-style'</pre></li><li data-uuid="cfef73b9-a772-4949-813b-fa11cd06c8ca"><pre>'border-bottom-style'</pre></li><li data-uuid="bf713c2e-a0a5-411e-9596-e9bb18156a1e"><pre>'border-left-style'</pre></li></ul><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>Border properties can be used in the following tags:</p><pre>h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, td, th, p, and div.</pre><p>For example, if you want to have a red border, type the following line of code, see the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_3.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_4.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Margin</h2><p>Margin properties specify the width of margin area within a box. The 'margin' property is a shorthand property for setting the margin of all four sides while the other margin properties only set their respective side. The value of the margin property is length (See <ac:link ac:anchor="Length Specification" /> for more details). Margin can be used in <em>RTF, ODT, ODP, DOCX</em> and <em>PPTX</em> report templates.</p><p>The margin properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:</p><ul><li data-uuid="bfd0bb0d-9489-4454-888f-362db8dc1635"><pre>'margin-top'</pre></li><li data-uuid="16757f78-1fc1-4d9e-bc88-3530bf955215"><pre>'margin-right'</pre></li><li data-uuid="0c447e04-0a8d-4095-a563-c49135008fdc"><pre>'margin-bottom'</pre></li><li data-uuid="f399dac7-aa5e-44b4-b00f-d494084c02a1"><pre>'margin-left'</pre></li></ul><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for margins are as follows:</p><pre>h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, table (supported only in RTF, ODT, and DOCX), p, and div.</pre><p>For example, if you want to set a specific margin width for your cells, type the lines of code as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_5.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_6.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Padding</h2><p>Padding properties specify the width of padding area within a box. The 'padding' property is a shorthand property for setting the padding for all four sides while the other padding properties only set their respective side. The value of the padding property is length (See <ac:link ac:anchor="Length Specification" /> for more details) adding can be used in <em>RTF, ODT</em> and <em>DOCX</em> report templates.</p><p>The padding properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:</p><ul><li data-uuid="90dc259f-88c6-4e0e-9a90-793492545e92">'padding-top'</li><li data-uuid="e69c2cba-3e2d-4822-8082-fb9221f48b3c">'padding-right'</li><li data-uuid="11e42977-a3bc-4ac7-a8a6-89d5485f9c3a">'padding-bottom'</li><li data-uuid="7afd48ca-19ef-459b-bb4d-350a26e1610f">'padding-left'</li></ul><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for padding are as follows:</p><pre>h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, td, th, p, and div.</pre><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1a21422-2bf3-4c82-b1b3-047b6b18433a"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="14a06f62-64d6-429b-9f93-48a6067fec4c">The supported border values of table tags for <em>RTF</em> documents are margin-left and margin-right.for more details.</li><li data-uuid="ff000129-81d3-40d8-92a8-29d8a7f30db4">The supported border values of table tags for <em>DOCX</em> documents are margin-left and margin-right.</li><li data-uuid="5435c3c5-a280-4e62-bbef-0fb8539b9268">The supported border values of all tags for <em>PPTX</em> documents are margin-left, margin-top and margin-bottom.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example, if you want to create specific padding styles for your cells, type the lines of code shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_7.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_8.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Color</h2><p>The 'color' property specifies the foreground color of an element's text contents. Only color values are allowed in this property (See Color Specification for more details on color).</p><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for colors are as follows:</p><pre>b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.</pre><p>For example, if you want to have a green text color, type the lines of code indicated in figure.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_9.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_10.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Display</h2><p>The 'display' property specifies how text will be displayed. The possible display values are:</p><ul><li data-uuid="d37f6978-178a-4b45-969b-d78c6bd1ec6b"><strong>block</strong>: This value will make an element appear in the output report.</li><li data-uuid="8d345801-3315-4353-b25e-2ea49c7ff7d7"><strong>none</strong>: This value will make an element disappear from the output report.</li></ul><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for the display property are:</p><pre>br, b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li (supported only in RTF, DOCX, and PPTX), table, tr, p, div, span, sup, and sub.</pre><p>For example, if you want to display an element but do not want to display the other(s), type the lines of code as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_11.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as indicated below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_12.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Font</h2><p>The 'font' property is a shorthand property for setting, except the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="5cf96792-679f-4e2c-a7a7-62218cf48ec5"><pre>'font family'</pre></li><li data-uuid="a3eeb0ce-3d79-40ab-adbe-54bda5e915f2"><pre>'font-style'</pre></li><li data-uuid="0cf4f461-cb57-4c11-93cc-bb3195ab6b14"><pre>'font-variant'</pre></li><li data-uuid="dc561b21-5ff9-4c19-8d3c-4f7e4807cee9"><pre>'font-weight'</pre></li><li data-uuid="6eac9e10-aaee-4afd-9395-4ec83484fc5b"><pre>'font-size'</pre></li></ul><h3>Font Family</h3><p>Specify a prioritized list of font family names and/or generic family names.</p><h3>Font Style</h3><p>Specify either normal or italic face (within the specified font family).</p><h3>Font Variant</h3><p>Specify either normal or small-caps of variant (within the specified font family). Font variant is not supported in <em>XLSX</em> report templates.</p><h3>Font Weight</h3><p>Specifies the weight of the font. The following values are defined:</p><ul><li data-uuid="3bd58b5f-1959-41f1-aa74-1c4f660f1016"><strong>normal</strong>, <strong>lighter</strong>, <strong>100</strong>, <strong>200</strong>,<strong> 300</strong>, and <strong>400</strong> will be rendered as ‘<strong>normal</strong>’.</li><li data-uuid="24252641-27b5-4d7a-934e-306e83cae05b"><strong>bold</strong>, <strong>bolder</strong>, <strong>500</strong>, <strong>600</strong>, <strong>700</strong>,<strong> 800</strong>, and<strong> 900</strong> will be rendered as ‘<strong>bold</strong>’.</li></ul><h3>Font size</h3><p>Specify the font size. Possible values include xx-small, x-small, small, medium, large, xx-large, and the integer number from 1 to 7.</p><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for the font property are as follows:</p><pre>b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.</pre><p>For example, if you want to have bold fonts in small caps, type the lines of code as displayed in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_13.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_14.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Text Align</h2><p>The 'text align' property describes how inline content of a block is aligned. Possible values of the text align property include: <strong>left</strong>, <strong>right</strong>, <strong>center</strong> and <strong>justify</strong>. The ‘text align’ property can be used in <em>RTF, ODT, ODP, DOCX,</em> and <em>PPTX</em> report templates.</p><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for the text align property are as follows:</p><pre>table, tr, td, th, p, and div.</pre><p>For example, if you want to align your paragraph to the left, center, and right, type the lines of code as shown in the Figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_15.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as displayed in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_16.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Text Transform</h2><p>The 'text transform' property controls capitalization effects of an element's text. Possible values of the 'text transform' property include:</p><ol><li data-uuid="59507ecf-6a16-4004-a2a0-80ccfe655b5a"><strong>capitalize<br /></strong>This will write the first character in an uppercase letter. Other characters will not be affected.</li><li data-uuid="2c7f5e69-cf23-4161-a47d-5439cde1ef1b"><strong>uppercase<br /></strong>This will write each word in upper case letters.</li><li data-uuid="dc1dcff0-bf30-467b-92ea-5a3e3a28ae44"><strong>lowercase<br /></strong>This will write each word in lower case letters.</li><li data-uuid="865d8471-210b-4c3e-96eb-51583e7414b7"><strong>none<br /></strong>This will not create any capitalization effects.</li></ol><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for the text transform property are as follows:</p><pre>b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.</pre><p>For example, if you want to make all letters in a paragraph become uppercases, type the line of code displayed in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_17.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_18.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>White-Space</h2><p>The 'white-space' property affects the vertical position of white-space inside a lined box. White-space can be used in <em>RTF, ODT, DOCX</em> and <em>PPTX</em> report templates. Possible values of the 'white-space' property include:</p><ol><li data-uuid="de6a1e0c-293b-4c6c-93ca-d7f0cecd590a"><strong>normal</strong> <br />To collapse sequences of white-space and break lines as necessary to fill line boxes.</li><li data-uuid="7bc3e5b0-f8aa-480d-892e-f801a46d2c1a"><strong>pre<br /></strong>To prevent from collapsing sequences of white-space. Lines are only broken at newlines in the source or at occurrences of &quot;\A&quot; in the generated content. Report Wizard will render this value as the &lt;pre&gt; tag.</li><li data-uuid="565d6d9a-6c56-46d2-a9c9-50525c9ac131"><strong>nowrap<br /></strong>To collapse white-space as for 'normal' but suppressing line breaks within text.</li><li data-uuid="d7416041-f51c-4a29-8718-57234d8943c6"><strong>pre-wrap<br /></strong>To prevent user agents from collapsing sequences of white-space. Lines are broken at newlines in the source, at occurrences of &quot;\A&quot; in the generated content, and as necessary to fill line boxes. This value will not be rendered as pre.</li><li data-uuid="54c3b793-86e7-49c7-a0b3-7cb756601b34"><strong>pre-line<br /></strong>To direct user agents to collapse sequences of white-space. Lines are broken at newlines in the source, at occurrences of &quot;\A&quot; in the generated content, and as necessary to fill line boxes. This value will not be rendered as pre.</li></ol><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for the 'white-space' property are as follows:</p><pre>b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.</pre><p>For example, if you want to preserve some white-space, type these lines of code as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="White-space Style Example" ac:alt="White-space Style Example"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_19.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_20.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Width</h2><p>The 'width' property specifies the contents width of each box generated by a block-level. The value of the width property is length (See <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Length Specification" /></span> for more details). Width can be used in <em>RTF, ODT,</em> and <em>DOCX</em> report templates.</p><p>The supported tag for the width property includes table.</p><p>For example, if you want to have a specific table width, type the lines of code as described in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Width Style Example" ac:alt="Width Style Example"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_21.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_22.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Text Decoration</h2><p>The 'text decoration' property describes decorations that are added to text. The possible values of the text decoration property are as follows:</p><ul><li data-uuid="c1a29f14-786c-4d25-bb9a-fdc9e1a3ef71"><strong>none<br /></strong>To produce no text decoration.</li><li data-uuid="a6937401-ea4c-4572-a848-d0f862f0ab96"><strong>underline<br /></strong>To underline each line of text.</li><li data-uuid="a9be719d-5b36-4003-a752-8c3a95181767"><strong>overline<br /></strong>To add a line above each line of text. </li><li data-uuid="70874423-9e5b-4fa4-8884-c9463cc17eea"><strong>line-through<br /></strong>To add a line that strikes through each line of text.</li><li data-uuid="cd3abf0c-56e7-483e-bdd7-e0d427e1f2e6"><strong>blink<br /></strong>To add the blinking effect to text (being visible and invisible alternatively). The blink text decoration supports only the <em>ODT</em> report template.<br /> </li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5435e63f-2782-46e4-a4bc-0cccf7deac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Report Wizard does not currently support the overline text decoration.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for the text decoration property are:</p><pre>b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.</pre><p>For example, if you want to create a line-through text, type the line of code as displayed in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_23.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_24.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Vertical Align</h2><p>The 'vertical align' property affects the vertical position of an element inside a lined box. This property can be used in <em>RTF, ODT</em> and <em>DOCX</em> report templates. Possible values of the vertical align property include:</p><ul><li data-uuid="725588bf-50fd-42b4-90cf-6b40d71327f1"><strong>baseline<br /></strong>This value will align the baseline of a box with the baseline of the parent box. Leave the vertical alignment to be default when you encounter this value.</li><li data-uuid="80e7c678-7d47-46e8-a9fb-44ec35250695"><strong>middle<br /></strong>This value will align the vertical midpoint of a box.</li><li data-uuid="22497f61-4476-4c83-a2bc-0152a3c86db4"><strong>top<br /></strong>This value will align the top of the aligned sub-tree with the top of a line box.</li><li data-uuid="b91cb349-fbd0-4d03-af01-7c4d13163dd4"><strong>bottom<br /></strong>This value will align the bottom of the aligned sub-tree with the bottom of a line box.</li></ul><p>The supported tags for the vertical align property are: table, tr, td, and th, for example, see figure below:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_25.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_26.png" /></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737700 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04073: (2026x) Supported file types

- page_id: `272737700`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737700/2026x+Supported+file+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:49:12.238+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Template Creation Tutorial > (2026x) Linking report templates to the modeling tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1351312429

1351312429

1351312429

Besides generating Rich Text Format templates and Rich Text Format reports, Report Wizard also generates other types of document:

1. *.html – Hyper Text Markup Language
2. *.rtf – Rich Text Format
3. *.odt – OpenDocument Text
4. *.ods – OpenDocument Spreadsheet
5. *.odp – OpenDocument Presentation
6. *.docx – Microsoft Word Document
7. *.xlsx – Microsoft Excel Spreadsheet
8. *.pptx – Microsoft PowerPoint Presentation
9. *.xml – Extensible Markup Language
10. *.txt – Text
11. *.docbook or *.dbk DocBook

To create a template for a specific type of document, simply open the editor designed for that particular type of document, and then save the template making sure that the filename extension is the same as that of the type of document.

1351312428

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

document:</p><ol><li><em>*.html</em> – Hyper Text Markup Language</li><li><em>*.rtf</em> – Rich Text Format</li><li><em>*.odt</em> – OpenDocument Text</li><li><em>*.ods</em> – OpenDocument Spreadsheet</li><li><em>*.odp</em> – OpenDocument Presentation</li><li><em>*.docx</em> – Microsoft Word Document</li><li><em>*.xlsx</em> – Microsoft Excel Spreadsheet</li><li><em>*.pptx</em> – Microsoft PowerPoint Presentation</li><li><em>*.xml</em> – Extensible Markup Language</li><li><em>*.txt</em> – Text</li><li><em>*.docbook</em> or <em>*.dbk</em> DocBook</li></ol><p>To create a template for a specific type of document, simply open the editor designed for that particular type of document, and then save the template making sure that the filename extension is the same as that of the type of document.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="08106878-41a7-41d4-a67b-2c218aaf396e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1351312428</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729234 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04074: (2026x) Supported HTML tags

- page_id: `272729234`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729234/2026x+Supported+HTML+tags
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:34:33.580+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Appendix D: HTML tag support
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1040445681

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1040445684

1040445678

**On this page**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1040445683

#### Font tags

A font tag consists of three attributes: **Size**, **Face**, and **Color**.

##### Size

The Size attribute determines the font size. Possible values are integers from 1 to 7. The default base font size is 3. The greater the value is, the larger the size becomes.

- The base font size for RTF documents is 24 dot (equivalent to 12 pt).
- The base font size for ODF documents is 12 pt.
- The base font size for OOXML documents is 12 pt.
- The base font size for HTML documents is determined by the web browser.

Each value will be multiplied by two. Shown below is an example of the size attribute:

```text
It will be rendered as font size 16 pt

It will be rendered as font size 12 pt

It will be rendered as font size 8 pt]]>
```

If the size attribute is specified without the face attribute, the default font will be determined by the template or document editor, unless the font tag is covered by other HTML elements, such as <code> or <tt>.

##### Face

The Face attribute defines the font name. If the face attribute is specified without the size attribute, the default size will be determined by the template or the document editor.

##### Color

The Color attribute specifies the text color. A color value can be either a hexadecimal number (prefixed with a hash mark) or one of the following sixteen colors. Colors are case-insensitive.

The table below lists several Font Colors:

| Color | Hexadecimal code |
| --- | --- |
| Black | #000000 |
| Silver | #C0C0C0 |
| Gray | #808080 |
| White | #FFFFFF |
| Maroon | #800000 |
| Red | #FF0000 |
| Purple | #800080 |
| Fuchsia | #FF00FF |
| Green | #008000 |
| Lime | #00FF00 |
| Olive | #808000 |
| Yellow | #FFFF00 |
| Navy | #000080 |
| Blue | #0000FF |
| Teal | #008080 |
| Aqua | #00FFFF |

For example:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As shown in the figure below, the outputs in *RTF, ODF,* or *HTML* will be:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Font style tag

The table below lists various Font Style Elements:

| Tag name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| TT | Renders teletyped or monospaced text. |
| I | Renders italic text. |
| B | Renders bold text. |
| BIG | Renders text in large font. |
| SMALL | Renders text in small font. |
| STRIKE and S | Renders strikethrough text. |
| U | Renders underlined text. |

- TT This tag will be rendered as <font face=”Courier New”>
- I This tag is supported by the existing HTML conversion component.
- B This tag is supported by the existing HTML conversion component.
- BIG This tag will be rendered as <font size=”5”>
- SMALL This tag will be rendered as <font size=”1”>
- STRIKE and S This tag will be rendered in a strikethrough text.
- U This tag is supported by the existing HTML conversion component.

#### Phrase elements

The table below lists assorted Phrase elements:

| Tag name | Function |
| --- | --- |
| EM | Indicates emphasis. |
| STRONG | Indicates stronger emphasis. |
| CITE | Contains a citation or a reference to other sources. |
| DFN | Indicates that this is the defining instance of the enclosed term. |
| CODE | Designates a fragment of computer code. |
| SAMP | Designates a sample output from programs, scripts, etc. |
| KBD | Indicates the text to be entered by the user. |
| VAR | Indicates an instance of a variable or program argument. |
| ABBR | Indicates an abbreviated form such as WWW, HTTP, URI, and Mass. |
| ACRONYM | Indicates an acronym such as WAC and radar. |

- *EM* 
This tag will be rendered as <i>
- *STRONG* 
This tag will be rendered as <b>
- *CITE* 
This tag will be rendered as <i>
- *DFN* 
This tag will be rendered as <i>
- *CODE* 
This tag will be rendered as <font face=”Courier New”>
- *SAMP* 
This tag will be rendered as <font face=”Courier New”>
- *KBD* 
This tag will be rendered as <font face=”Courier New”>
- *VAR* 
This tag will be rendered as <i>
- *ABBR* 
This tag will be rendered as normal text.
- *ACRONYM* 
This tag will be rendered as normal text.

#### Ordered and unordered lists and list Item tags

Ordered and unordered lists are rendered in an identical manner; however, ordered list items are numbered.

The report engine supports both unordered and ordered lists without attributes. The list tag attributes will be ignored in the report output. The list tag attributes are type, start, value, and compact.

Neither unordered nor ordered lists are supported in *XLSX* and *ODS* templates.

##### Ordered lists

An Ordered List is defined by the <OL> element. This element contains one or more <LI> elements that define the actual items of the list.

Unlike unordered lists (UL), items in an ordered list have a definite sequence. A conversion will render each item in the list with a number. All <OL> attributes will be ignored in the report output. An example of an Ordered List tag is shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As shown in the figure below, the outputs in *RTF, ODF,* or *HTML* will be:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Nested ordered lists

HTML conversion will indent nested lists with respect to the current level of nesting. Each level will begin at 1. An example of the Nested Ordered List tag is shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As shown in the figure below, the outputs in*RTF, ODF,* or *HTML* will be:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Unordered lists

An Unordered List is defined by the UL element. This element contains one or more LI elements that define the actual list items.

A conversion will render the UL element with a bullet preceding each list item. All UL attributes will be ignored in the report output. The figure below shows an example of an Unordered List tag:

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
As shown in the figure below, the outputs in *RTF, ODF*, or *HTML* will be:

##### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Nested unordered lists

Lists can also be nested. *HTML* conversion will indent nested lists with respect to the current level of nesting.

*HTML* conversion should attempt to present a small filled-in circle to the first level, a small circle outline to the second level, and a filled-in square to the third level. Bullets after the third level are filled-in squares. An example of the Nested Unordered List tag is shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As shown in the figure below, the outputs in*RTF, ODF,* or *HTML* will be:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Definition list tags

A Definition List is defined by the DL element. An entry in the list is created using the DT element for the term being defined and the DD element for the definition of the term.

A definition list can have multiple terms for a given definition, as well as multiple definitions for a given term. Authors can also give a term without a corresponding definition, and vice versa, but such a structure rarely makes sense.

A conversion will render DT as a non-indent item and DD as a single indent item. The Definition List tag is not supported in the*XLSX* template. The figure below shows an example of a Definition List tag:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As shown in the figure below, the outputs in *RTF, ODF* or *HTML* will be:

#### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
Line and paragraph tags

A line break is defined by the <BR> element. This element inserts a single line break. It is an empty tag, meaning that it has no end tag. The line attributes will be ignored in the report output.

A paragraph is defined by the <P> element. The element automatically creates some space before and after itself. The paragraph attributes will be ignored in the report output. The figure below shows an example of a Line Break and Paragraph tag:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As shown in the figure below, the outputs in *RTF, ODF,* or *HTML* will be:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Preformatted text

A preformatted text is defined by the <PRE> element. All the space and carriage returns are rendered exactly as you type them. The preformatted attributes will be ignored in the report output. The figure below shows an example of the preformatted text:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As shown in the following two figures, the outputs in *RTF/ ODF* and *HTML* will be, respectively:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Heading tags

A heading is defined by the <H1>, <H2>, <H3>, <H4>, <H5>, or <H6> element. In this report, all heading tags will be rendered as <b> for*ODT, RTF,* and *OOXML* document outputs. The heading attributes will be ignored in the report output. The figure below shows an example of Heading tags.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As shown in the following two figures, the outputs in *RTF / ODF* and *HTML* will be, respectively:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Link tags

A Link tag is defined by the <A> element. This element is used to create a link to another document with the href attribute. The href attribute specifies the destination of the link. Link tags are not supported in the *XLSX* template. The figure below shows an example of a Link tag:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As shown in the following three figures, the outputs in *RTF, ODF*, and *HTML* will be, respectively:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Table tags

A table is defined by the <TABLE> element. A table consists of multi-dimensional data arranged in rows and columns.

##### Table elements

The <TABLE> element takes a number of optional attributes to provide presentational alternatives in a document. The table attributes will be ignored in the report output, except for the following attributes:

- border

Specifies the width in unit of the border of a table.

- bgcolor

Specifies the table background color. Applying the background color here will affect the whole table.

Table elements are not supported in *XLSX, PPTX, ODS,* and *ODP* templates.

The figure below shows an example of table tags:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As shown in the following two figures, the outputs in *RTF / ODF* and *HTML* will be, respectively:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Border**

Border width in *HTML* is specified in pixels. When the table attributes are converted into *RTF*, *ODF*, or *OOXML*, 1 pixel will be equal to 1 pt.

**Color**

The attribute value type "bgcolor" refers to color definitions as specified in [SRGB]. A color value may be either a hexadecimal number (prefixed by a hash mark) or one of the following sixteen colors. Colors are case-insensitive.

The following table lists common Table Element Colors.

| Color | Hexadecimal Code |
| --- | --- |
| Black | #00000000 |
| Silver | #C0C0C0 |
| Gray | #808080 |
| White | #FFFFFF |
| Maroon | #800000 |
| Red | #FF0000 |
| Purple | #800080 |
| Fuchsia | #FF00FF |
| Green | #008000 |
| Lime | #00FF00 |
| Olive | #808000 |
| Yellow | #FFFF00 |
| Navy | #000080 |
| Blue | #0000FF |
| Teal | #008080 |
| Aqua | #00FFFF |

##### Row elements

The <TR> elements act as a container for a row of table cells. The <TR> elements must be contained within a <TABLE>.

<TR> contains <TH> or <TD> elements, which in turn contain the actual data of the table. <TR> takes presentational attributes for specifying the alignment of cells within the row and the row's background color. The row attributes will be ignored in the report output, except for the following attributes:

- align - Specifies the horizontal alignment for each cell in a row.
- valign - Specifies the vertical position of a cell's content.
- bgcolor - Specifies the table background color. A background color will apply to rows only (see Color in Table Elements for more details).

Row elements are not supported in *XLSX, PPTX, ODS*, and *ODP* templates.

**Align**

This attribute specifies the alignment of data and the justification of text in a cell. Possible values are:

- left - Left-flushed data/Left-justified text. This is the default value for table data.
- center - Centered data/Center-justified text. This is the default value for table headers.
- right - Right-flushed data/Right-justified text.
- justify - Double -justified text.
- char - No text alignment set.

**Valign**

This attribute specifies the vertical position of data within a cell. Possible values are:

- top: Cell data is flush with the top of a cell.
- middle: Cell data is centered vertically within a cell. This is the default value.
- bottom: Cell data is flush with the bottom of a cell.
- baseline: No text alignment set.

The figure below shows an example of TR tags:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As shown in the following two figures, the outputs in *RTF / ODF* and *HTML* will be, respectively:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Cell elements

The <TD> elements define a data cell in a table. <TD> elements are contained within a <TR> element (a table row). The cell attributes will be ignored in the report output, except for the following attributes:

- align specifies the horizontal alignment for each cell in the row. See Align in Row Elements for more details.
- valign specifies the vertical position of a cell's contents. See Valign in Row Elements for more details.
- bgcolor specifies the table background color. A background color will apply only to cells. See Color in Table Elements for more details.
- rowspan rows spanned by the cell
- colspan columns spanned by the cell

Cell elements are not supported in *XLSX, PPTX, ODS,* and *ODP* templates.

**Row span**

This attribute specifies the number of rows spanned by the current cell. The default value of this attribute is one ("1"). For an *RTF* output, the result of row span (**.rtf*) is readable only in Word on Mac, and MS Word.

**Column span**

This attribute specifies the number of columns spanned by the current cell. The default value of this attribute is one ("1"). The figure below shows an example of a column span:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As shown in the following two figures, the outputs in *RTF / ODF* and *HTML* will be, respectively:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Column width

Table column width can be adjusted via attributes and stylesheet. For example, a column's width can be set by its width attribute. Percentage or pixel units are allowed for each <TD> element. You may set table width in <table> tags by absolute pixel value or percentage of page ratio, then allocate width from the table to each column later.

For example:

```text
25%
                75%
       


        
                400px
                100px
        
 



	
		30px
		60px
		90px
	

 

	
		25%
		75%
	




Using Stylesheet:


	
		70%
		30%
	


]]>
```

DOCX, RTF and ODT templates retain their width values.The column width in the report is the same as defined in the Advanced HTML Editor dialog.

If the defined values exceed the paper margins, the table column widths will be converted to a percentage format so that the table appears within the page margins.

##### Header elements

The <TH> elements define a header cell in a table. <TH> elements are contained within a <TR> element (a table row). The header attributes will be ignored in the report output, except for the following attributes:

- align specifies the horizontal alignment for each cell in the row. See Align in Row Elements for more details.
- valign specifies the vertical position of a cell's contents. See Valign in Row Elements for more details.
- bgcolor specifies the table background color. A background color will apply to the whole table. See Color in Table Elements for more details.
- rowspan rows spanned by the cell. See Row span in Cell Elements for more details.
- colspan columns spanned by the cell. See Column span in Cell Elements for more details.

The default alignment for <TH> is center and the default font style for <TH> is bold. Header elements are not supported in *XLSX, PPTX, ODS,* and *ODP* templates. The figure below shows an example of header elements:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The following two figures show the outputs in *RTF / ODF* and *HTML,* respectively.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Image tags

The <img> tag embeds an image in a document. The <img> tag consists of four attributes: **src**, **width**, **height**, and**alt**.

##### src

The src attribute specifies the location of an image resource. The value of this attribute can be one of the following types:

- A URL. The recognized scheme types are HTTP, HTTPS , and FILE
- An absolute path such as c:/user/image.png . The path separator can be either /(slash) or \(blackslash)

The output image format will depend on the format of the image source.

##### width

The width attribute specifies the width of an image in pixel units. For example, width = “100” or width = “100px”

##### height

The **height** attribute specifies the height of an image in pixel units. For example, height = “100”, or height = “100px”

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If the width or height attribute of an image is not specified, the size of the image will be calculated according to the following rules:

- For an image file that contains the width and height properties, such as JPG, PNG, and GIF , the size of the image output will be calculated from the size of the image.
- For an image file that has no width and height properties, such as SVG, EMF, and WMF , the size of the image output will be calculated from the size of the paper.

##### alt

The **alt** attribute specifies the alternate text of an image in case the image cannot be displayed.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- The alternate text of image tags in DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX is available only in Microsoft Office.
- The alternate text of image tags in RTF files is shown only in the Description field, rather than the Title field in Microsoft Word and OpenOffice Writer.
- The alternate text of image tags in RTF files is not supported in LibreOffice.

In the event that the HTML code is as follows:

```text
]]>
```

The image will appear as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Superscript and subscript tags

##### Superscript

The <sup> tag defines a supersript, which is a very small letter, figure, or symbol, and printed above the line. Superscript characters can be used to write footnotes, like www[1], for example:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Subscript

The <sub> tag defines a subscript, which is a very small letter, figure, or symbol, and printed below the line. For example, subscript characters can be used to write chemical formulas, such as H2O:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b608afa2-4fdd-48f2-a2ac-a42195152d13"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1040445681</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="49ee684a-c861-4346-a035-6dbfdf9d186e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1040445684</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f704c75-5916-43c5-9e66-f165604f4ad9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1040445678</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c0840865-c991-4a54-a531-0d9bdd567983" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cef9e852-9d62-4a72-b2b3-07679b3b9679"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1040445683</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h2>Font tags</h2><p>A font tag consists of three attributes: <strong>Size</strong>, <strong>Face</strong>, and <strong>Color</strong>.</p><h3>Size</h3><p>The Size attribute determines the font size. Possible values are integers from 1 to 7. The default base font size is 3. The greater the value is, the larger the size becomes.</p><ul><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The base font size for <em>RTF</em> documents is 24 dot (equivalent to 12 pt).</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The base font size for <em>ODF</em> documents is 12 pt.</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The base font size for <em>OOXML</em> documents is 12 pt.</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The base font size for <em>HTML</em> documents is determined by the web browser.</li></ul><p>Each value will be multiplied by two. Shown below is an example of the size attribute:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="061c8f2c-d588-47f1-86c8-59ead78096ad"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<font size="5">
It will be rendered as font size 16 pt
<font size="3">
It will be rendered as font size 12 pt
<font size="1">
It will be rendered as font size 8 pt]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>If the size attribute is specified without the face attribute, the default font will be determined by the template or document editor, unless the font tag is covered by other HTML elements, such as &lt;code&gt; or &lt;tt&gt;.</p><h3>Face</h3><p>The Face attribute defines the font name. If the face attribute is specified without the size attribute, the default size will be determined by the template or the document editor.</p><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Color</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Color attribute specifies the text color. A color value can be either a hexadecimal number (prefixed with a hash mark) or one of the following sixteen colors. Colors are case-insensitive.</span></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>The table below lists several Font Colors:</p><table class="wrapped"
````
