<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735776 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04107: (2026x) SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description

- page_id: `272735776`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735776/2026x+SvcV-10b+Services+State+Transition+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:51.951+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-10b-services-state-transition-description', 'svcv-10b', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SvcV-10b is a graphical method of describing a resource (or function) response to various events by changing its state. The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the resources in the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

The explicit time sequencing of service functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description. SvcV-10b can be used to describe the explicit sequencing of the service functions. Alternatively, SvcV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the actions internal to a single service function, or the sequencing of service functions with respect to a specific resource.

The intended usage of the SvcV-10b includes:

- Definition of states, events, and state transitions (behavioral modeling).
- Identification of constraints.

**Implementation**

SvcV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SvcV-10b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of service functions described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='']. However, the relationship between the actions included in SvcV-10b and the functions in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space=''] depends on the purposes of the Architectural Description and the level of abstraction used in the models. The explicit sequencing of functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='']. SvcV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the functions, the sequencing of actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of functions with respect to a specific resource.

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SvcV-10b is a graphical method of describing a resource (or function) response to various events by changing its state. The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the resources in the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.</p><p>The explicit time sequencing of service functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description. SvcV-10b can be used to describe the explicit sequencing of the service functions. Alternatively, SvcV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the actions internal to a single service function, or the sequencing of service functions with respect to a specific resource.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-10b includes:</p><ul><li data-uuid="91054eed-7816-45a5-86b5-76db0393ff70">Definition of states, events, and state transitions (behavioral modeling).</li><li data-uuid="da2cb3a6-030c-4a9f-8293-592441929227">Identification of constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description" ac:alt="SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="svcv_10b.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The SvcV-10b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of service functions described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /></ac:link>. However, the relationship between the actions included in <span class="confluence-link">SvcV-10b</span> and the functions in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> depends on the purposes of the Architectural Description and the level of abstraction used in the models. The explicit sequencing of functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /></ac:link>. SvcV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the functions, the sequencing of actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of functions with respect to a specific resource.</p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="c4270c12-6480-4092-8397-40f1db12709a"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="State" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="2498da2e-0df2-4909-8bd0-a1813942d663"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735772 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04108: (2026x) SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description

- page_id: `272735772`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735772/2026x+SvcV-10c+Services+Event-Trace+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:51.370+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-10c-services-event-trace-description', 'svcv-10c', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SvcV-10c provides a time ordered examination of the interactions between services functional resources. Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

The SvcV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of service functions and service data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or Service Port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.

The intended usage of the SvcV-10c includes:

- Analysis of resource events impacting operation.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional system requirements.

**Implementation**

SvcV-10c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SvcV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description' space=''] to describe the dynamic behavior of resources. The data content of messages that connect Resource Flows in a SvcV-10c model may be related, in modeling terms, with Resource Flows (interactions, in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix' space='']), Resource Flows (data, in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='']) and information elements (in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) DIV-3 Physical Data Model' space='']) modeled in other models.

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Message' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Method' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Resource Exchange in SvcV-10c diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SvcV-10c provides a time ordered examination of the interactions between services functional resources. Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.</p><p>The SvcV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of service functions and service data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or Service Port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-10c includes:</p><ul><li data-uuid="3d1d7c1c-2bf8-464a-aab3-0f1698e560b1">Analysis of resource events impacting operation.</li><li data-uuid="e4292aad-7f71-4814-b563-d246c2f9b0be">Behavioral analysis.</li><li data-uuid="b86fe7f3-5928-45c3-a064-43a640e66934">Identification of non-functional system requirements.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-10c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description" ac:alt="SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="svcv_10c.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The SvcV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description" /></ac:link> to describe the dynamic behavior of resources. The data content of messages that connect Resource Flows in a SvcV-10c model may be related, in modeling terms, with Resource Flows (interactions, in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-1 Services Context Description" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix" /></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix" /></ac:link>), Resource Flows (data, in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /></ac:link><span class="confluence-link"> </span>) and information elements (in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) DIV-3 Physical Data Model" /></ac:link>) modeled in other models.</p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="3189a1bc-ee87-4961-b8cd-45bbc3d88652"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Interaction" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="84fc3438-7e7b-4912-b30e-a0c4a2d8bd14"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="f06e2cca-f0a3-478b-b2ee-b1cbc918aee6"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service Interface" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="a878f9b8-0e9f-4552-8064-25ca21716372"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service Message" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="784122c8-fddd-4ce9-982d-eebd8198b7eb"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service Method" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="9b9f0db2-913e-4a23-aeb8-9a653c4ee891"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating Resource Exchange in SvcV-10c diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735783 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04109: (2026x) SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description BPD

- page_id: `272735783`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735783/2026x+SvcV-10c+Services+Event-Trace+Description+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:53.560+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</p><p>SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</p>
````

## PAGE 04110: (2026x) SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description

- page_id: `272735519`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735519/2026x+SvcV-2+Services+Resource+Flow+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:28.463+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-2-services-resource-flow-description', 'svcv-2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

395159322

395159324

395159323

**Description**

A SvcV-2 specifies the Resource Flows between Service Specificationsand may also list the protocol stacks used in connections.

A SvcV-2 DoDAF-described Model is used to give a precise specification of a connection between Service Specifications. This may be an existing connection or a specification of a connection that is to be made for a future connection.

The intended usage of the SvcV-2 includes:

- Resource Flow specification.

**Implementation**

SvcV-2 can be represented using:

- SvcV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- SvcV-2 Internal Block diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Any protocol referred to in a SvcV-2 diagram needs be defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) StdV-1 Standards Profile' space='']StdV-1 Standards Profile.

395159321

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735494 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04111: (2026x) SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix

- page_id: `272735494`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735494/2026x+SvcV-3a+Systems-Services+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:27.034+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-3a-systems-services-matrix', 'svcv-3a', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

395570067

395570069

395570068

**Description**

A SvcV-3a enables a quick overview of all the system-to-service resource interactions specified in one or more [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space=''] models. The SvcV-3a provides a tabular summary of the system and services interactions specified in the SvcV-1 Services Context Description for the Architectural Description. This model can be useful in support existing systems that are transitioning to provide services. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies).

The SvcV-3a can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of system-to-service interactions in context with the architecture’s purpose.

The intended usage of the SvcV-3a includes:

- Summarizing system and service resource interactions.
- Interface management.
- Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.

**Implementation**

SvcV-3a can be represented using a SvcV-3a diagram which is non editable Dependency Matrix.

The SvcV-3a provides a tabular summary of the systemand servicesinteractions specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='']. Each Resource Exchangelisted in the SvcV-3a table should be traceable to Operational Exchange listed in the corresponding [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='']. Each Resource Exchangemay relate to a known Function (from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='']) that produces or consumes it. DataElements as well as the other type of Resource ExchangeItems are exchanged by Service Providers and Service Requesters represented in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='']SV-6 table.

395570066

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating SvcV-3a matrix' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a32230f2-0d09-4ad4-b288-326f915958d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">395570067</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="92c4fa50-5c07-4225-8432-736d29cf1b0b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">395570069</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89aa1592-3f3b-4fe9-92f3-ec52837282d6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">395570068</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A SvcV-3a enables a quick overview of all the system-to-service resource interactions specified in one or more <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-1 Services Context Description" /></ac:link> models. This model can be useful in support existing systems that are transitioning to provide services. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies).</p><p>The SvcV-3a can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of system-to-service interactions in context with the architecture’s purpose.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-3a includes:</p><ul><li>Summarizing system and service resource interactions.</li><li>Interface management.</li><li>Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-3a can be represented using a SvcV-3a diagram which is non editable Dependency Matrix.</p><p>The SvcV-3a provides a tabular summary of the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">system </span>and <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">services </span></span>interactions specified in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-1 Services Context Description" /></ac:link>. Each Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>listed in the SvcV-3a table should be traceable to Operational Exchange listed in the corresponding <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix" /></ac:link>. /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) that produces or consumes it. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Data </span>Elements as well as the other type of Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>Items are exchanged by Service Providers and Service Requesters represented in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">SV-6 table</span></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735534 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04112: (2026x) SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix

- page_id: `272735534`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735534/2026x+SvcV-3b+Services-Services+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:29.706+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-3b-services-services-matrix', 'svcv-3b', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

396077917

396077919

396077918

**Description**

A SvcV-3b enables a quick overview of all the services resource interactions specified in one or more SvcV-1 Services Context Description models. The SvcV-3b provides a tabular summary of the services interactions specified in the SvcV-1 Services Context Description for the Architectural Description. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies). In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services as an input to the SvcV-10a Services Rules Model, SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description, and SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description, implemented as orchestrations of services.

The SvcV-3b can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of service pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose. One type of organization is a Service Hierarchy or Taxonomy of Services.

The intended usage of the SvcV-3b includes:

- Summarizing service resource interactions.
- Interface management.
- Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.

**Implementation**

SvcV-3b can be represented using a SvcV-3b diagram which is non editable Dependency Matrix.

This model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services as an input to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-10a Services Rules Model' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description' space=''], implemented as orchestrations of services.

396077916

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating SvcV-3b matrix' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The SvcV-3b provides a tabular summary of the services interactions specified in the SvcV-1 Services Context Description for the Architectural Description. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies). In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services as an input to the SvcV-10a Services Rules Model, SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description, and SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description, implemented as orchestrations of services.</p><p>The SvcV-3b can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of service pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose. One type of organization is a Service Hierarchy or Taxonomy of Services.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-3b includes:</p><ul><li>Summarizing service resource interactions.</li><li>Interface management.</li><li>Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-3b can be represented using a SvcV-3b diagram which is non editable Dependency Matrix.</p><p>This model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services as an input to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-10a Services Rules Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV- 10a Services Rules Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description" /></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description" /></ac:link>, implemented as orchestrations of services.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2284c6e9-45c1-4164-ab73-42370aceb193"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">396077916</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service Exchange" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating SvcV-3b matrix" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735528 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04113: (2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description

- page_id: `272735528`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735528/2026x+SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:29.282+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-4-services-functionality-description', 'svcv-4', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

396204750

396204752

396204751

**Description**

The SvcV-4 DoDAF-described Model addresses human and service functionality.

The primary purpose of SvcV-4 is to:

- Develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource.
- Ensure that the service functional connectivity is complete (i.e., that a resource’s required inputs are all satisfied).
- Ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail.

The Services Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the:

- Allocation of service functions to resources.
- Flow of resources between service functions.

The SvcV-4 is the Services Viewpoint counterpart to the OV-5b Operational Activity Model of the Operational Viewpoint.

The intended usage of the SvcV-4 includes:

- Description of task workflow.
- Identification of functional service requirements.
- Functional decomposition of Services.
- Relate human and service functions.

**Implementation**

SvcV-4 can be represented using:

- SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='svcv_4.png']
- SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram which is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='svcv_4_flow.png']

The SvcV-4 is the behavioral counterpart to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space=''] (in the same way that [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space=''] is the behavioral counterpart to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='']).

396204748

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function Action' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Process Flow Diagrams From Compositions or Aggregations Defined in Process Diagrams' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735556 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04114: (2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description BPD

- page_id: `272735556`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735556/2026x+SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:30.959+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</p><p>SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735552 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04115: (2026x) SvcV-5 Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `272735552`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735552/2026x+SvcV-5+Services+to+Operational+Activity+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:30.648+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-5-operational-activity-to-services-traceability-matrix', 'svcv-5', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SvcV-5 addresses the linkage between service functions described in SvcV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5a Operational Activity Decomposition Tree or OV-5b Operational Activity Model. The SvcV-5 depicts the mapping of service functions (and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them) to operational activities and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a service solution. During requirements definition, the SvcV-5 plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system function requirements to those associated with user requirements.

The intended usage of the SvcV-5 includes:

- Tracing service functional requirements to user requirements.
- Tracing solution options to requirements.
- Identification of overlaps or gaps.

**Implementation**

SvcV-5 can be represented using:

- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with SvcV-5 matrix' space=''] describes the mapping between Service s and Operational Activities directly or using the implied relations.

DoDAF uses the term Operational Activity in the OVs and the term Service Function in the SVs to refer to essentially the same kind of thing—both activities and service functions are tasks that are performed, accept inputs, and develop outputs. The distinction between an Operational Activity and a Service Function is a question of what and how. The Operational Activity is a specification of what is to be done, regardless of the mechanism used. A Service Function specifies how a resource carries it out. For this reason, the SvcV-5 is a significant model, as it ties together the logical specification in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space=''] with the physical specification of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='']. Service Functions can be carried out by Resources.

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Is Capable To Perform' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Supports' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SvcV-5 addresses the linkage between service functions described in SvcV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5a Operational Activity Decomposition Tree or OV-5b Operational Activity Model. The SvcV-5 depicts the mapping of service functions (and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them) to operational activities and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a service solution. During requirements definition, the SvcV-5 plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system function requirements to those associated with user requirements.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-5 includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing service functional requirements to user requirements.</li><li>Tracing solution options to requirements.</li><li>Identification of overlaps or gaps.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-5 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Working with SvcV-5 matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-5 Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">describes the mapping between </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Service</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">s and Operational Activities directly or using the implied relations.</span></li></ul><p>DoDAF uses the term Operational Activity in the OVs and the term Service Function in the SVs to refer to essentially the same kind of thing—both activities and service functions are tasks that are performed, accept inputs, and develop outputs. The distinction between an Operational Activity and a Service Function is a question of what and how. The Operational Activity is a specification of what is to be done, regardless of the mechanism used. A Service Function specifies how a resource carries it out. For this reason, the SvcV-5 is a significant model, as it ties together the logical specification in the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ OV-5a Operational Activity Decomposition Tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b Operational Activity Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with the physical specification of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /></ac:link>. Service Functions can be carried out by Resources.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Is Capable To Perform" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service Function" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Supports" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="3e634151-caf8-4f10-a97d-222f92b26569" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735558 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04116: (2026x) SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `272735558`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735558/2026x+SvcV-5+Services+to+Service+Contracts+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:31.299+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix** depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,and the Columns are Service Contracts.

To create an SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services or their specializations);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Service Contracts);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Services or their specializations map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.

To mapServices or their specializations to Service Contracts

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix</strong> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,</span><span> and the Columns are Service Contracts</span><span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>To create an SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify</span><strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services or their specializations);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify </span><strong>Column Scope</strong> (Service Contracts);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper" style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span>.</span></li></ol><p> Services <span>or their specializations</span> map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map<span> Services or their specializations to Service Contracts </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol>
````

- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:11.038+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix** (SvcV-6) specifies the characteristics of the data exchanged between service roles.

##### Adding existing Service Exchanges

To add an existing Service Exchange

1. In the Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Elements dialog opens.
2. Double-click Service Exchange or Service Connector from the list to add them to the table. For multiple selection, click the **Multiple Selection** button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Service Exchange / Service Connector and click **+** button.In case a Service Connector is selected, all the Service Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.
3. Click OK when you are done.

- Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurements cells are editable in the table.
- Other cells are read-only.
- To create and modify the Service Interactions, you can use Services Structure (Sv-Sr) diagram.

##### Generating Reports

The Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix has a predefined report available. You can generate the report:

- Directly from the table toolbar, clicking the **Report**button,
- Using a quick report option from the Containment tree
- Through the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Report Wizard' space='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix report

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The<strong> Select Elements</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Double-click Service Exchange or Service Connector from the list to add them to the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48f021b3-2cf1-43b8-8ca7-31a54cac7bdd"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>For multiple selection, click the <strong>Multiple Selection</strong> button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Service Exchange / Service Connector and click <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>In case a Service Connector is selected, all the Service Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurements cells are editable in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Other cells are read-only.</span></li><li>To create and modify the Service Interactions, you can use Services Structure (Sv-Sr) diagram.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Generating Reports</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix has a predefined report available. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735843 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04118: (2026x) SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix

- page_id: `272735843`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735843/2026x+SvcV-6+Services+Resource+Flow+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:13.362+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**The SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix** addresses service exchanges between services.

You can add any available kind (such as Information Exchange, Materiel Exchange, Organizational Exchange, Energy Exchange) of Service Exchanges in this table.

##### Adding new Service Exchanges

To add new Service Exchange

1. In the SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Services Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Service Exchanges are added to the table.

##### Adding existing Service Exchanges

To add an existing Service Exchange

1. In the SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Service Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Service Exchanges by clicking the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Service Exchanges are added to the table.

- Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are editable in the table.
- Other cells are read-only.
- To create and modify the Service Exchanges, you can use Services Taxonomy (Sv-Tx) diagram.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Service Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables you to analyze and manage the Service Exchange data.

##### Adding Service Functions

To add Service Functions

1. In the SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix, click a Service Exchange row for which you want to add Service Functions.
2. In the table toolbar, click the Service Functions button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space=''] dialog opens.
3. In the open dialog, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space=''] .
4. Click OK .

##### Generating Reports

The Services Resource Flow Matrix has a predefined report available. You can generate the report:

- Directly from the table toolbar, clicking the **Report**button,
- Using a quick report option from the Containment tree
- Through the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Report Wizard' space='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Services Resource Flow Matrix report

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 04119: (2026x) SvcV-7 Services Measures Matrix

- page_id: `272735860`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735860/2026x+SvcV-7+Services+Measures+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:17.162+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-7-services-measures-matrix', 'svcv-7', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

396906475

396906478

396906477

**Description**

The SvcV-7 depicts the measures (metrics) of resources. The Services Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in an SvcV-1 Services Context Description by depicting the characteristics of the resources in the SvcV-1 Services Context Description.

In addition, this model is useful in support of the net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. Service measures for Service Level Agreements for each service and may include a number of service consumers, service usage by consumers, and the minimum, average and maximum response times, allowed downtime, etc. Measures of interest for a Chief Information Office or Program manager may include measures that assess service reuse, process efficiency, and business agility.

The intended usage of the SvcV-7 includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measures (metrics).
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

SvcV-7 can be represented using:

- SvcV-7 Typical measures table [ATTACHMENT filename='svcv_7_typical.png']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='']
- SvcV-7 Actual measures table [ATTACHMENT filename='svcv_7_actual.png']
- SvcV-7 actual measures spreadsheet report

## PAGE 04120: (2026x) SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description

- page_id: `272735852`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735852/2026x+SvcV-8+Services+Evolution+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:15.891+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-8-services-evolution-description', 'svcv-8', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

397044898

397044900

397044899

**Description**

The SvcV-8 presents a whole lifecycle view of resources (services), describing how it changes over time. It shows the structure of several resources mapped against a timeline.

In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. This model can present a timeline of services evolve or are replaced over time, including services that are internal and external to the scope of the architecture.

The intended usage of the SvcV-8 includes:

- Development of incremental acquisition strategy.
- Planning technology insertion.

**Implementation**

The SvcV-8 view can be represented using a SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735761 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04121: (2026x) SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast

- page_id: `272735761`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735761/2026x+SvcV-9+Services+Technology+and+Skills+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:49.620+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-9-services-technology-and-skills-forecast', 'svcv-9', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

397238836

397238838

397238837

**Description**

The SvcV-9 defines the underlying current and expected supporting technologies and skills. Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills, and expected improvements or trends. New technologies and skills are tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description model milestones and linked to Capability Phases.

The SvcV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the architecture. The SvcV-9 provides descriptions of relevant:

- Emerging capabilities.
- Industry trends.
- Predictions (with associated confidence factors) of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software services.
- Current and possible future skills.

In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and services, the SvcV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture. Given the future-oriented nature of this model, forecasts are typically made in short, mid and long-term timeframes, such as 6, 12 and 18-month intervals. In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. As technologies change, like incorporation of Representational State Transfer (REST) services in the Web Services Description Language, this model can present a timeline of technologies related services over time.

The intended usage of the SvcV-9 includes:

- Forecasting technology readiness against time.
- HR Trends Analysis.
- Recruitment Planning.
- Planning technology insertion.
- Input to options analysis.

**Implementation**

SvcV-9 can be represented using a SvcV-9 table.

The specific time periods selected (and the trends being tracked) can be coordinated with architecture transition plans (which the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description' space=''] can support). That is, insertion of new capabilities and upgrading or re-training of existing resources may depend on or be driven by the availability of new technology and associated skills.

If standards are an integral part of the technologies important to the evolution of a given architecture, then it may be convenient to combine SvcV-9 with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) StdV-2 Standards Forecast' space=''] into a composite Fit-for-Purpose View.

397238834

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forecast' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating SvcV-9 table' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="064d0dc8-cbab-4bf8-9916-f59f20267a05"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">397238836</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b69b244-d21e-4e70-9d39-a2894a478e9e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">397238838</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c32e261c-90e3-4b5a-8bd1-29a9ec885cbc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">397238837</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SvcV-9 defines the underlying current and expected supporting technologies and skills. Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills, and expected improvements or trends. New technologies and skills are tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description model milestones and linked to Capability Phases.</p><p>The SvcV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the architecture. The SvcV-9 provides descriptions of relevant:</p><ul><li>Emerging capabilities.</li><li>Industry trends.</li><li>Predictions (with associated confidence factors) of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software services.</li><li>Current and possible future skills.</li></ul><p>In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and services, the SvcV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture. Given the future-oriented nature of this model, forecasts are typically made in short, mid and long-term timeframes, such as 6, 12 and 18-month intervals. In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. As technologies change, like incorporation of Representational State Transfer (REST) services in the Web Services Description Language, this model can present a timeline of technologies related services over time.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-9 includes:</p><ul><li>Forecasting technology readiness against time.</li><li>HR Trends Analysis.</li><li>Recruitment Planning.</li><li>Planning technology insertion.</li><li>Input to options analysis.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-9 can be represented using a SvcV-9 table.</p><p>The specific time periods selected (and the trends being tracked) can be coordinated with architecture transition plans (which the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description" /></ac:link> can support). That is, insertion of new capabilities and upgrading or re-training of existing resources may depend on or be driven by the availability of new technology and associated skills.</p><p>If standards are an integral part of the technologies important to the evolution of a given architecture, then it may be convenient to combine SvcV-9 with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) StdV-2 Standards Forecast" /></ac:link> into a composite Fit-for-Purpose View.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2532cc1e-f9ae-408f-99b9-a4448f990bca"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">397238834</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Forecast" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating SvcV-9 table" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734474 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04122: (2026x) Swapping

- page_id: `272734474`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734474/2026x+Swapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:56.393+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Refactoring
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Swapping allows counter-change symbols with each other on the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can swap the symbols in the following diagrams:

- In the Activity diagram, you can swap actions, merge or decision nodes, join or fork nodes with each other, if the symbols are connected through control flow.
- In the State diagram, you can swap states or pseudo states (initial state, deep history, join, fork, junction, choice, entry point, etc.) with each other, if symbols are connected through transition.

To swap elements, do one of the following

- Drag one shape onto another. Swap does not work when dragged shape can be added into the target shape (or in other words, can be added as a child).
- On the diagram, select the two shapes that you want to swap, right-click, and from the shortcut menu, select Refactor > Swap .

- Swapping does not work when symbols have different parents.
- If you cannot swap within a Teamwork Cloud project, make sure you have the right to edit the model of this project and then try to lock for edit this diagram.

In the following example, you can see the cases when you can swap the elements and the cases when you cannot swap the elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Rule | Example |
| --- | --- |
| You can swap the elements that are in the same branch | You can swap the following elements:“Select the reading item to remove” with “Confirm deletion”“Request removal confirmation” with “Check if there are loaned copies of reading item”“Check if there are loaned copies of reading item” with “Notify librarian and do not allow removal”“Check if there are loaned copies of reading item” with “Check if reading item is reserved” |
| You cannot swap the elements that have different parents | You cannot swap the following elements:“Select the reading item to remove” with Request removal confirmation, because one element is in the “Librarian” partition and the other is in the “System” partition.“Confirm deletion” with “Check if there are loaned copies of reading item” because one element is in the “Librarian” partition and the other is in the “System” partition. |
| You cannot swap the elements that are in different branches | You cannot swap the following elements:“Notify librarian and do not allow removal” with “Check if reading item is reserved”. |

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Refactoring' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with model elements' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731450 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04123: (2026x) Swimlane

- page_id: `272731450`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731450/2026x+Swimlane
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:59.081+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Functional analysis > (2026x) Modeling functional flows with Activities
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1404690412

1404690415

1404690414

INLINE

Actions and subactivities can be organized into Swimlanes in the Activity diagrams. Swimlanes are used to organize responsibility for actions and subactivities according to class. They often correspond to the organizational units in a business model.

Swimlanes limit and provide a view of the behaviors invoked in the activities. They consist of one or more partitions and can be vertical or horizontal.

An Activity diagram can be visually divided into Swimlanes, each separated from the neighboring Swimlanes by vertical or horizontal solid lines on both sides. Each Swimlane represents a responsibility for part of the overall activity, and may eventually be implemented by one or more objects. The relative ordering of the Swimlanes has no semantic significance, but can indicate some affinity. Each action is assigned to one Swimlane. Transitions can cross lanes. The routing of a transition path is non-essential.

You can customize Swimlane representation by changing its symbol property values. Press Alt + Enter to open the appropriate dialog. Each property is described in the description area on this dialog.

To create a Swimlane

1. On the Activity diagram palette, click the **Vertical Swimlanes** button [IMAGE alt='' src='']and then click an empty space on the diagram pane.
2. If the **Represent Properties** dialog opens, select the properties you want to display as Swimlanes in the diagram (see the following figure) and click **OK**. Otherwise, a few empty Swimlanes are created. The **Represent Properties** dialog opens if:The Activity diagram is owned by a classifier, such as a Class, Component, Use Case.The classifier or its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying a Use Case subject' space='MED'] has properties.Partitions are created for each property you select to represent as a Swimlane in the Activity diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can also create a Swimlane by dragging one or more properties, Actors, Classes, or Instance Specifications from the Containment tree to the Activity diagram pane. Partitions for each element are created as well.

To set representative elements, do one of the following

- Open the Specification window of the Swimlane and click the cell of the Represents property value. Then click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] and select the element. Click OK when you are done.
- From the Containment tree, drag the representative element to the partition on the diagram.

To add an additional partition, do one of the following

- On the diagram, right-click the Swimlane, select Insert Swimlane and then Insert Vertical Swimlane or Insert Horizontal Swimlane .
- From the Containment tree, drag one or more property, actor, class or instance specification elements to the Swimlane on the diagram.

To draw multidimensional Swimlanes

1. Draw a vertical Swimlane.
2. From the Swimlane shortcut menu, select Insert Horizontal Swimlane .
3. Insert as many horizontal and vertical Swimlanes as you need.

To add model elements to a Swimlane

- If a Swimlane is already drawn in the Activity diagram, drawing an action (or any other element) highlights the Swimlane in blue. This means the action shape depends on the Swimlane symbol. If the model elements depend on a Swimlane symbol, they are deleted if the Swimlane symbol is deleted. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

To rearrange Swimlanes, do one of the following

- Drag any of the Swimlanes in the direction where you want it to be.
- Click the heading of any Swimlane and use the displayed control arrow to move the Swimlane left, right, up or down. [ATTACHMENT filename='move_swimlane_left_button.png']

To delete a single Swimlane entirely, do one of the following

- Click the header of the Swimlane, then press Delete.
- Click the header of the Swimlane, then press Ctrl + D.
- Right-click the header or any empty space on the Swimlane, then select Delete Swimlane .

To delete all Swimlanes and their contents

- Click any empty space on the Swimlanes, then press Ctrl + D.

To delete Swimlanes without deleting contained elements, do one of the following

- Click any empty space on the Swimlanes, then press Delete.
- Right-click the headers or anywhere on the Swimlanes, then select Delete All Swimlanes, Keep Contents .

1404690411

**Rrelated pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Formatting symbols' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activity diagram' space='']

model elements depend on a Swimlane symbol, they are deleted if the Swimlane symbol is deleted.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of multidimensional swimlane" ac:alt="Example of multidimensional swimlane"><ri:attachment ri:filename="multidimensional_swimlane_example.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To rearrange Swimlanes, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Drag any of the Swimlanes in the direction where you want it to be.</li><li>Click the heading of any Swimlane and use the displayed control arrow to move the Swimlane left, right, up or down.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Example of 'Move Swimlane Left' Control" ac:alt="Example of 'Move Swimlane Left' Control"><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_swimlane_left_button.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To delete a single Swimlane entirely, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the header of the Swimlane, then press Delete.</li><li>Click the header of <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739893 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04124: (2026x) Switching between projects with different AF related perspectives

- page_id: `272739893`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739893/2026x+Switching+between+projects+with+different+AF+related+perspectives
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:52:11.043+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A particular behavior is applied when switching between projects with different Architecture Framework (AF) related perspectives.When you work with two projects using different perspectives than those projects were created with and switch between them, a message is displayed.You can choose whether to change the perspective according to the project's framework or the latest used perspective in that message.

In the following table, see the cases of the actions required when switching between projects A and B.

| Actions of Project A | Project A perspective according to AF | The last used perspective of Project A | Actions of Project B | Project B perspective according to AF | The last used perspective of Project B | Required actions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Create Project A | UAF | - | Create project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically. (In the example below, Case #1) |
| Open Project A | UAF | - | Open project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically. (In the example below, Case #1) |
| Migrate/Open/Create Project A | UAF | - | Migrate project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Create Project A | UAF | - | Create project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Open project A | UAF | - | Open project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Migrate/Open/Create Project A | UAF | - | Migrate project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Create Project A | UAF | MODAF | Create project B | MODAF | - | No actions |
| Open Project A | UAF | MODAF | Open project B | MODAF | - | No actions |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | UAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the UAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to UAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | - | Switch to opened project B | NAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the NAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to NAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | NAF | DoDAF 2 | The project you are opening requires the NAF perspective.To continue, choose one of the following:OK to switch the perspective to NAF (according to the framework).Cancel to switch the perspective to DoDAF 2(the latest used perspective).(In the example below, Case #2) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | MODAF | DoDAF 2 | The project you are opening requires the MODAF perspective.To continue, choose one of the following:OK to switch the perspective to MODAF (according to the framework).Cancel to switch the perspective to DoDAF 2 (the latest used perspective).(In the example below, Case #2) |

##### Examples of messages

Here are examples of messages you receive when switching projects with different perspectives.

**Case #1.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Case #2.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739745 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04125: (2026x) Switching to Business Modeling Perspectives

- page_id: `272739745`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739745/2026x+Switching+to+Business+Modeling+Perspectives
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:44:08.023+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The business modeling perspectives are dedicated to business process modelers. The perspectives offer a simplified user interface by showing the features that are relevant to business process modeling and hiding the others that are not.

Business Modeler offers two business modeling perspectives. They are as follow:

- Business Analyst perspective that provides set of most often used elements of BPMN standard.
- Business Architect perspective that provides all BPMN elements

To switch to the Business Architect or Business Analyst perspective

1. From the main menu, click Options > Perspectives > Perspectives . The Select Perspectives dialog will open.
2. Select Business Analyst or Business Architect appropriately and click Apply .

**Related resources**

- Customizing and Selecting Perspective

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

They are as follow:</p><ul><li data-uuid="c54866ba-8fc0-4767-a825-0c3d5ace4649">Business Analyst perspective that provides set of most often used elements of BPMN standard.</li><li data-uuid="2ddb7eb8-9539-4cad-b58a-3ae5b7896137">Business Architect perspective that provides all BPMN elements</li></ul><p>To switch to the Business Architect or Business Analyst perspective</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="15da3e87-f130-416e-a6a1-3c87bd7edaa3">From the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Perspectives</strong> &gt; <strong>Perspectives</strong>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734684 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04126: (2026x) Symbol Properties dialog

- page_id: `272734684`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734684/2026x+Symbol+Properties+dialog
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:42:46.305+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Formatting symbols
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the **Symbol Properties** dialog, you may define various symbol (or group of symbols) properties, such as fill color, font type, whether to wrap text. The **Symbol Properties** dialog is a non-modal window - you can edit the symbol properties and see the changes on the diagram instantly. In addition, while the dialog is active you can still work with diagrams or other dialogs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Symbol Properties** dialog

1. On the diagram, select a symbol or a group of symbols.
2. Do one of the following:
  - From the main menu, select Edit > Symbol > Symbol Properties .
  - From the selected elements' shortcut menu, choose Symbol Properties .
  - Press Alt+Enter.

In the following table, you will find the described common functions that **Symbol Properties** dialog provides.

| Icon | Title | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Apply Style | Button | Click to open the menu with the available . |
|  | Show All Properties | Button | Click to display all the properties for the selected symbol types.Note that the Show All Properties button is displayed only when at least two shapes of different element types are selected, for example, a class and a package. |
|  | Make Default | Button | Click to change the default symbol properties to the properties that are specified in this dialog.WarningIt is highly recommended to use the Make Default button only if you fully understand the symbol styles and how your symbol styles can be affected.WarningThe Make Default button is available in the Teamwork Cloud project only if you have locked the symbol styles for edit. |

If your project is of the earlier version you need to set up a new symbol properties manually.

**Related pages**

- Symbol Properties dialog
- Applying image
- Displaying icon or image
- Symbol styles option group
- Working with Symbol styles
- Properties Inheritance
- Stereotype properties
- Copy and paste symbol style
- Making your own default style for new projects

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734644 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04127: (2026x) Symbol styles option group

- page_id: `272734644`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734644/2026x+Symbol+styles+option+group
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:12.230+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Formatting symbols
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All symbols in the modeling tool are created according to active properties styles. There may be more than one property style in the same project, and the whole style may be applied for the project.

Every style has its own presentation of Diagram, Shape, Path, and Stereotype that you can modify using the **Project Options** dialog in the **Symbol styles** option group. You can set your own options for every model element to the current style.

**Path**, **Shape**, and **Stereotype** branches have the inner structure that helps you find the model element, the representation of which must be changed. The section on the right side of the dialog contains possible choices and instruments to manage them.

The following properties are defined for the formatting symbols:

- Shapes . Set general options for the shapes in the right pane of the Project Options dialog. You can set options for all shapes that appear on the Diagram pane.
- Paths . Set general options for the paths in the right pane of the Project Options dialog. You can set options for all paths that appear on the Diagram pane.
- Diagram . Set general options about a diagram.
- Stereotypes . Set general options for the stereotypes in the right pane of the Project Options dialog. You can set options for all stereotypes that may be applied to elements on the Diagram pane.

##### Changing options for multiple symbols

To change properties for multiple symbols, press Ctrl or Shift and select few symbols in the **Project Options** dialog > **Symbol styles** option group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Shape, path, and diagram options

All shapes, paths and diagrams that can be created in the project, are listed in the **Project Options** dialog. If the Shape, Path, or Diagram branches are selected in the tree, the general properties can be set in the right pane of this dialog.

When expanding any of these option groups, the style for a particular element (diagram) can be created.

To apply a new style to a previously created element symbol

1. In the Project Options dialog , change the element style properties and click the Apply button. The Select Diagrams dialog opens.
2. Select the diagrams to which the element properties will be applied and click OK . The Select Properties to Apply dialog opens.
3. Select the properties to be applied to the element symbol by moving them from the All list to the Selected list.
4. Click **OK** when you are done. If a new style was set, it will be applied for all newly created elements after drawing them on the diagram pane. You can always set the default symbols style to the element by clicking the **Apply Default Symbol Style** button in the main toolbar.

##### Properties extension by diagram

Diagram, shape, path, and stereotype properties can be extended by the particular diagram type. This means that presentation style options will be applied only for the specified element symbol in the specific diagram.

To specify element properties for the selected diagram type

1. In the Project Options dialog, the Symbol Styles option group, do one of the following
  - Selected the desired element and from its shortcut menu, select the diagram type. The diagram is added as an additional branch to the section. [ATTACHMENT filename='extend_by_diagram_1.png']
  - Click the Extend by Diagram button, in the open Extend by Diagram dialog, click the Add Diagram button and select the desired diagram type.
2. Set the style properties for the element. The properties will be applied only in the specified type of diagram.

To remove the extended diagram from the tree, do one of the following

- In the Project Options dialog, select the extended diagram and from the shortcut menu, choose Remove .
- In the Project Options dialog, select the extended diagram and click the Remove button on the right.

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Formatting symbols' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
