<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730622 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04167: (2026x) Template Management Pane

- page_id: `272730622`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730622/2026x+Template+Management+Pane
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:37:19.945+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Report Wizard dialog
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1272375443

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1272375448

1272651476

**On this page**

3

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1272375446

Report Wizard uses templates to generate reports. Each report file format depends on the type of template files. The type of template files that Report Wizard supports includes *plain text, RTF, HTML, Office Open XML* *(ISO/IEC 29500:2008)*, *OpenDocument format (ISO/IEC 26300)*, and *XML template (DocBook or FO)*.

Report Wizard provides predefined templates such as Use Case, Model Extension, Data Dictionary, IEEE 1233, Class Specification Diagram, Business Process Diagram, and Web Publisher templates. The report template management pane in the **Report Wizard** dialog lists all of the report templates from which you can select to manage or generate your report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Template options, which are highlighted in orange, are available to choose from in the Report Wizard dialog.

#### Using the Template Management Pane

In this pane, you can create a new template, or use an existing one. The following table displays the buttons from which you can choose while working with your template. Additionally, the control buttons in the lower part of the dialog [IMAGE alt='' src=''],**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**, and [IMAGE alt='' src=''] help you cancel an action, go to the next step, and generate a report.

| Button | Function |
| --- | --- |
|  | Opens the New Template dialog through which you can create a new template. |
|  | Edits a template and saves it in Report Wizard. |
|  | Deletes a template from Report Wizard. |
|  | Opens a template file in the default editor. |
|  | Creates a new template variable and modify or delete an existing template variable in the Template Variable dialog. |
|  | Clones a selected template. |
|  | Copies a selected template and adds it to the project file. |
|  | Extracts an attached template to a local report templates folder. |
|  | Imports a template. |
|  | Exports a template. |

#### Working with a Template

The following instructions illustrate how to use each button in the Report Wizard to create, select, and manage a template.

To create a template

1. On the main menu, click Tools > Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **New Template** dialog will open. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Creating a new template in the New Template dialog.
3. Enter the template name and description.
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='button-1.png'] to select the location of the new template.
5. Select the template file location and click [ATTACHMENT filename='Select_button.png'] .
6. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Create_button.png'] . The template file will be created in the Select Template pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The template file is created in the Select Template pane.

To edit a template

1. In the**Report Wizard** dialog, select a template and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Edit Template** dialog will appear. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Editing a template in the Edit Template dialog.
2. Edit the template name, description, category, and locate the template file’s location.
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Save_button.png'] .

To delete a template

1. In the Report Wizard dialog, select a template and click [ATTACHMENT filename='Delete_button.png'] . The Confirm delete dialog will appear.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Yes_button.png'] to delete the selected template from the template list, or click [ATTACHMENT filename='No_button.png'] to cancel the operation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Confirm delete dialog.

To clone a template

1. In the **Report Wizard** dialog, select a template and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Clone Template** dialog will appear. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Cloning a template in the Clone Template dialog.
2. Enter the name and description. The name of the cloned template should begin with **Copy of**(name of template).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Create_button.png'] to clone the template.

To copy a template and add it to the project file

1. Select a template in the Report Wizard dialog.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Attach_button.png'] to make a copy of the selected template and add it to the project’s files, so anyone with access to the project can generate a report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The steps to attach a template to a project file.

The new template will be created and displayed in the **Report Wizard** dialog and the Containment tree. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Result of attaching a template in the Report Wizard dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Result of attaching a template in the Containment tree.

You can right-click the attached template in the Containment tree, and select **Generate Report{attached template name}** to open the **Report Wizard** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening the Report Wizard dialog via attached template in the Containment tree.

#### INFO: Note

Note

- In the Report Wizard dialog, when you select the attached template, the [ATTACHMENT filename='Attach_button.png'] button will be changed to [ATTACHMENT filename='Extract_button.png'] button.
- You can use the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to extract the attached template to the local report template folder. After you finish editing, you can use the [ATTACHMENT filename='Attach_button.png'] button to re-attach the updated report template to your project.

To extract a template

1. Select an attached template in the Report Wizard dialog.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Extract_button.png'] to extract the attached template to the local report template folder.
3. If the template already exists in the local report template folder, a confirmation dialog will be displayed. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The confirmation dialog when you extract a template.
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Yes_button.png'] to extract the template. The attached template will be extracted to a local template.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A local template in the Report wizard's template selection pane.

- After you finish editing, you can use the [ATTACHMENT filename='Attach_button.png'] button to re-attach the updated report template to your project.
- You can remove an attached template from a project by selecting it and clicking [ATTACHMENT filename='Delete_button.png'] .

To import a template

1. In the **Report Wizard** dialog, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Select Location** dialog will appear. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting the location to import a template.
2. Select a Report Wizard template with the filename extension **.mrzip* and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The following **Message** dialog will open. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Message dialog of a successful import.

To export a template

1. In the **Report Wizard** dialog, select a template and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Export** dialog opens. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting a location to export a template.
2. Select a directory or folder location into which you want to export the template, followed by an entered file name. NoteIf the exported template needs related files to be included, select the **Includes other files** option. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select one or multiple files from the list box. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Including related files to an exported mrzip file.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Message** dialog opens.
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [ATTACHMENT filename='Message Dialog.png'] The Message dialog of successful export. NoteThe selected template will be exported together with included related files. When imported to the modeling tool, the included files will be in the **includedfiles** folder in the report directory.[IMAGE alt='' src='']Related files included in the includedfiles folder when imported to the modeling tool.

To open a template file in the default editor

- In the Report Wizard dialog, select a template and click [ATTACHMENT filename='Open_button.png'] . The template file will open in the default editor.

#### Working with a template variable

A template contains variables and their values. You can create a new template variable, and modify or delete an existing template variable in the **Template Variable** dialog. You cannot modify the variables of a built-in template. You can only create or modify a template variable of a user-created template.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Template Variable dialog.

The **Template Variable** dialog, contains the following:

- Variable pane: This pane consists of a table in which the first column is the variable name, the second column is the variable value, and also includes a report description field. You can view and directly edit the value of a selected variable in the second column.
- Control buttons: [ATTACHMENT filename='OK_button.png'] and [ATTACHMENT filename='Cancel_button.png'] .

The following instructions explain how to manage the variables of a template.

To create a template variable

1. Select a template in the **Report Wizard** dialog and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Template Variable** dialog will open.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **New Variable** dialog will appear. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Creating a new variable in the New Variable dialog.
3. Enter the variable name (no space between characters) and description.
4. Select an owner for this new variable from the **Owner** drop-down list (optional).
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. You will see the newly-created variable’s name and value in the **Variable** pane.
6. In the **Template Variables** dialog, click**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**.

To modify a variable value in the Template Variable dialog

1. Select a template in the **Report Wizard** dialog, and click **Variable** to open the **Template Variable** dialog.
2. Double-click the **Variable** value column and modify the value in the column. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Editing a variable value in the Template Variable dialog.

To modify a value in the Template Variable value dialog

1. Double-click the **Variable** value column in the **Variable** pane in the **Template Variable** dialog, and click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The **Variable**value dialog will open. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Editing a variable value in the Variable value dialog.
2. To modify the variable value, you can enter the value of a report variable either in plain text or HTML by selecting the HTML check box in the **Variable Value** dialog. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Entering a report variable value in HTML.
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='OK_button.png'] .
4. When you finish modifying variables and their values in the Template Variable dialog, click either [ATTACHMENT filename='OK_button.png'] to confirm the changes or [ATTACHMENT filename='Cancel_button.png'] to discard them.

To delete a variable

1. Select a variable in the Template Variable dialog and click [ATTACHMENT filename='Delete_button.png'] . The Question dialog will appear prompting you to confirm before deleting the selected variable.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']****to delete the selected variable. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Deleting a variable.

#### Glossary

- Use case A a kind of behavior classifier that specifies a complete unit of useful functionality performed by one or more subjects to which the use case applies in collaboration with one or more actors. It also, for complete use cases, yields an observable result that is of some value to those actors or each subject. Use cases allow you to capture the requirements of systems under design or consideration, describe functionality provided by those systems, and determine the requirements those systems pose on their environment.

- Business process diagram A road map for implementation. It outlines the expected outcome, and provides something concrete from which to build.

#### More information about working with templates in Report Wizard

To learn more about templates and template variables, you can visit the following pages:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Report Wizard template language' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Report Wizard Environment options' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Template variables' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Report Wizard template editor' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d6cfca7-b4df-4678-a5f2-1d8d6f278792"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1272375443</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9e273fb-dd12-419a-a765-fa064265385d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1272375448</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8738cde-a82f-4511-b45c-cf3e4fe0e6f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1272651476</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b3502a6-f30a-4392-a5aa-ba374f05e586"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="73610c64-c40b-4597-9dc6-e9a19d386a7d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1272375446</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Report Wizard uses templates to generate reports. Each report file format depends on the type of template files. The type of template files that Report Wizard supports includes <em>plain text, RTF, HTML, Office Open XML</em> <em>(ISO/IEC 29500:2008)</em>, <em>OpenDocument format (ISO/IEC 26300)</em>, and <em>XML template (DocBook or FO)</em>.</p><p>Report Wizard provides predefined templates such as Use Case, Model Extension, Data Dictionary, IEEE 1233, Class Specification Diagram, Business Process Diagram, and Web Publisher templates. The report template management pane in the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog lists all of the report templates from which you can select to manage or generate your report.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Wizard Dialog Template Management.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Template options, which are highlighted in orange, are available to choose from in the Report Wizard dialog.</h6><h2>Using the Template Management Pane</h2><p>In this pane, you can create a new template, or use an existing one. The following table displays the buttons from which you can choose while working with your template. Additionally, the control buttons in the lower part of the dialog <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Back_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>,<strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong>, and <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> help you cancel an action, go to the next step, and generate a report.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><h4><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h4></div></td><td>Opens the <strong>New Template</strong> dialog through which you can create a new template.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><h4><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h4></div></td><td>Edits a template and saves it in Report Wizard.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><h4><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Delete_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h4></div></td><td>Deletes a template from Report Wizard. </td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><h4><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Open_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h4></div></td><td>Opens a template file in the default editor.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><h4><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Variable_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h4></div></td><td>Creates a new template variable and modify or delete an existing template variable in the <strong>Template Variable</strong> dialog.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="clone.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td>Clones a selected template.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><h4><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Attach_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></h4></div></td><td><p>Copies a selected template and adds it to the project file.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Extract_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td>Extracts an attached template to a local report templates folder.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><h4><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Import_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h4></div></td><td colspan="1">Imports a template.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><h4><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Export_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h4></div></td><td colspan="1">Exports a template.</td></tr></tbody></table><h2><span>Working with a Template</span></h2><p>The following instructions illustrate how to use each button in the Report Wizard to create, select, and manage a template.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a template</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong>. The <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog will open.</li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong>New Template</strong> dialog will open.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="NewTemplate_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);">Creating a new template in the New Template dialog.</span></h6></li><li>Enter the template name and description.</li><li>Click<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="button-1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to select the location of the new template.</li><li>Select the template file location and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The template file will be created in the <strong>Select Template</strong> pane.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Wizard Dialog Select Template Pane.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>The template file is created in the Select Template pane.</h6><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>To edit a template</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In the<strong> Report Wizard</strong> dialog, select a template and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong>Edit Template</strong> dialog will appear.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Edit Template Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);">Editing a template in the Edit Template dialog.</span></h6></li><li>Edit the template name, description, category, and locate the template file’s location.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Save_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target">To delete a template</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog, select a template and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Delete_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong>Confirm delete</strong> dialog will appear.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Yes_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><strong> </strong>to delete the selected template from the template list, or click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="No_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to cancel the operation.</li></ol><p>    <ac:image ac:title="Confirm Delete Dialog " ac:alt="Confirm Delete Dialog "><ri:attachment ri:filename="confirm-delete.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The Confirm delete dialog.</h6><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>To clone a template</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog, select a template and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="clone.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong>Clone Template</strong> dialog will appear.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Clone Template Dialog" ac:alt="Clone Template Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="clone-report-a.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Cloning a template in the Clone Template dialog.</h6></li><li><p>Enter the name and description. The name of the cloned template should begin with <strong>Copy of </strong>(name of template).</p></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to clone the template.</li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>To copy a template and add it to the project file</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a template in the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Attach_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to<span> make a copy of the selected template and add it to the project’s files, so anyone with access to the project can generate a report.</span></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Wizard Dialog Selecting Template.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The steps to attach a template to a project file.</h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The new template will be created and displayed in the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog and the Containment tree.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Wizard Dialog Attaching Template.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Result of attaching a template in the Report Wizard dialog.</h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Containment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Result of attaching a template in the Containment tree.</h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">You can right-click the attached template in the Containment tree, and select <strong>Generate Report{attached template name}</strong> to open the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="GenerateReport_menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Opening the Report Wizard dialog via attached template in the Containment tree.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2408ecb-313c-4cc5-ad74-26558ef910a6"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>In the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog, when you select the attached template, the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Attach_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button will be changed to <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Extract_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</li><li>You can use the <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Extract_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong> button to extract the attached template to the local report template folder. After you finish editing, you can use the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Attach_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button to re-attach the updated report template to your project.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>To extract a template</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an attached template in the<strong> Report Wizard</strong> dialog.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Extract_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to extract the attached template to the local report template folder.</li><li>If the template already exists in the local report template folder, a confirmation dialog will be displayed.<br /><p><ac:image ac:title="Confirmation dialog when you perform extract template" ac:alt="Confirmation dialog when you perform extract template"><ri:attachment ri:filename="question-b.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>The confirmation dialog when you extract a template.</h6></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Yes_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to extract the template. The attached template will be extracted to a local template.</li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Wizard Dialog Local Template.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;">A local template in the Report wizard's template selection pane.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="52011f7e-5e88-4e8d-a1f3-463d2e5a49ca"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span>After you finish editing, you can use the </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Attach_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span> button to re-attach the updated report template to your project.</span></li><li>You can remove an attached template from a project by selecting it and clicking <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Delete_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To import a template</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Import_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong>Select Location</strong> dialog will appear.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Select Location Dialog" ac:alt="Select Location Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select-location-dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Selecting the location to import a template.</h6><p><br /></p></li><li><p>Select a Report Wizard template with the filename extension <em>*.mrzip</em> and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Open_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The following <strong>Message</strong> dialog will open.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="message_import.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>The Message dialog of a successful import.</h6></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To export a template</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog, select a template and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Export Button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong>Export</strong> dialog opens.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Export Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Selecting a location to export a template.</h6></li><li><p>Select a directory or folder location into which you want to export the template, followed by an entered file name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef93ac17-e8dc-45da-a185-85b1b5d81501"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the exported template needs related files to be included, select the <strong>Includes other files</strong> option.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Export Dialog - Browse.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Browse Button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select one or multiple files from the list box.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Export Dialog - Selecting Files.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);">Including related files to an exported mrzip file.</span></h6></li><li><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Export Button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong>Message</strong> dialog opens.</span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);">Click <span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK Button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.<br /></span></p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Message Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><h6><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);">The Message dialog of successful export.</span></h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f43ec906-ae25-4e59-b085-4d8f36c333c7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The selected template will be exported together with included related files. When imported to the modeling tool, the included files will be in the <strong>includedfiles</strong> folder in the report directory.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Includedfiles Folder.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Related files included in the includedfiles folder when imported to the modeling tool.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);"><br /></span></p></li></ol><p>To open a template file in the default editor</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog, select a template and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Open_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The template file will open in the default editor.</li></ul><h2>Working with a template variable</h2><p>A template contains variables and their values. You can create a new template variable, and modify or delete an existing template variable in the <strong>Template Variable</strong> dialog. You cannot modify the variables of a built-in template. You can only create or modify a template variable of a user-created template.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="TemplateVariable_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Template Variable dialog.</h6><p>The <strong>Template Variable</strong> dialog, contains the following:    </p><ul><li><strong>Variable</strong> pane: This pane consists of a table in which the first column is the variable name, the second column is the variable value, and also includes a report description field. You can view and directly edit the value of a selected variable in the second column.</li><li>Control buttons: <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ul><p>The following instructions explain how to manage the variables of a template.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a template variable</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Select a template in the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Variable_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong>Template Variable</strong> dialog will open.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong>New Variable</strong> dialog will appear.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="NewVariable_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);">Creating a new variable in the New Variable dialog.</span></h6></li><li><p>Enter the variable name (no space between characters) and description.</p></li><li><p>Select an owner for this new variable from the <strong>Owner</strong> drop-down list (optional).</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. You will see the newly-created variable’s name and value in the <strong>Variable</strong> pane.</p></li><li><p>In the <strong>Template Variables</strong> dialog, click<strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong>.</p></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>To modify a variable value in the Template Variable dialog</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Select a template in the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog, and click <strong>Variable</strong> to open the <strong>Template Variable</strong> dialog.</p></li><li><p>Double-click the <strong>Variable</strong> value column and modify the value in the column.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="TemplateValue_inTemplateVariable_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);">Editing a variable value in the Template Variable dialog.</span></h6></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>To modify a value in the Template Variable value dialog</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Double-click the <strong>Variable</strong> value column in the <strong>Variable</strong> pane in the <strong>Template Variable</strong> dialog, and click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="button-1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The <strong>Variable </strong>value dialog will open.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Variablevalue_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);">Editing a variable value in the Variable value dialog.</span></h6></li><li><p>To modify the variable value, you can enter the value of a report variable either in plain text or HTML by selecting the HTML check box in the <strong>Variable Value</strong> dialog.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="HTMLoption _inVariablevalue_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);">Entering a report variable value in HTML.</span></h6></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);"> </span></li><li>When you finish modifying variables and their values in the <strong>Template Variable</strong> dialog, click either <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to confirm the changes or <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><strong> </strong>to discard them.</li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>To delete a variable</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a variable in the <strong>Template Variable</strong> dialog and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Delete_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong>Question</strong> dialog will appear prompting you to confirm before deleting the selected variable.</li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Yes_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><strong> </strong>to delete the selected variable.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Deleting A Variable" ac:alt="Deleting A Variable"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-report-variable-e.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Deleting a variable.</h6></li></ol><h2>Glossary</h2><ul><li><strong>Use case<br /></strong>A a kind of behavior classifier that specifies a complete unit of useful functionality performed by one or more subjects to which the use case applies in collaboration with one or more actors. It also, for complete use cases, yields an observable result that is of some value to those actors or each subject. Use cases allow you to capture the requirements of systems under design or consideration, describe functionality provided by those systems, and determine the requirements those systems pose on their environment.</li></ul><ul><li><strong>Business process diagram<br /></strong>A road map for implementation. It outlines the expected outcome, and provides something concrete from which to build.</li></ul><h2>More information about working with templates in Report Wizard</h2><p class="confluence-link">To learn more about templates and template variables, you can visit the following pages:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Report Wizard template language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Report Wizard Template Language]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Report Wizard Environment options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Report Wizard Environment Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template variables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Template Variables]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Report Wizard template editor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Report Wizard Template Editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737613 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04168: (2026x) Template tool

- page_id: `272737613`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737613/2026x+Template+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:48:50.236+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Helper tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050315270

2050315282

2050315268

**On this page**

43

2050315271

$template is the tool used for getting a template’s information.

****

###### **$template.getName()**

Return the name of a template.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | Return a template name. |

****

###### **$template.getResourcesLocation()**

Return the folder location of the resource file.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | The location of a resource file folder. |

****

###### **$template.getTemplateFile()**

Return a template filename.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | Return a template file name. |

****

###### **$template.getTemplateLocation()**

In case, there is an included template, the latest included template location would be returned, otherwise, the main location of a template file will be returned.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | The location of the latest included template file folder. |

****

###### **$template.getMainTemplateLocation()**

Return the folder location of the main template file.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | The location of a main template file folder. |

****

###### **$template.getOutputFile()**

Return the output filename.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | Return an output file name. |

****

###### **$template.getOutputFileNoExt()**

Return the output name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An output name without a file name extension. |

###### **$template.getOutputLocation()**

Return the folder location of the output file.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | Return the location of an output file folder. |

###### **$template**.getReportDataVariables()****

Return Report Data variables from the selected Report Data.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of Report Data variables. |

For example:

```text

```

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735220 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04169: (2026x) Template variables

- page_id: `272735220`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735220/2026x+Template+variables
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:06.256+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1267789098

1267789101

1267789100

The variables imported to the template are collected by the type of element selected in the package scope. Use the fourth step in the **Report Wizard** dialog to select the package scope. In this example, we take a class diagram with the class name Customer, see figure below, to print it in a report.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Class Diagram: Customer.

To print attributes of the Customer class in a report

- Right-click the Customer class diagram and open the Specification dialog. The element type will appear on the dialog title, and the attribute names will appear on the right-hand side of the dialog box (see the following figure).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Specification dialog.

With the following VTL code, you can print the attributes of the Class element:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

```text

```

The table below gives and explains the additional properties which are not part of the UML specifications, but retrievable by Report Wizard.

| Element Owner | Property Name | Function |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Diagram | image | To return the diagram’s image. |
| Diagram | diagramType | To return the diagram’s type. |
| Element | image | To generate the element’s image or to print an empty text if the element does not refer to any diagram. |
| Element | elementType | To return a name of an element’s type (metaclass / stereotype) in lowercase letters and without spaces. For example:return "usecase" for a Use Casereturn "class" for a Classreturn "callbehavioraction" for a Call Behavior Actionreturn "flowport" for a Flow Port (a stereotype defined in SysML v1) |
| Element | humanName | To return an element’s human readable name, which is a string concatenation between the human element type and the element’s name. |
| Element | humanType | To return an element’s type in a human readable format. |
| Element | appliedStereotype | To return an applied stereotype of an element. |
| Element | activeHyperlink | To return an active hyperlink of an element. |
| Element | hyperlinks | To return all hyperlinks attached to an element. |
| Element | toDo | To return an element’s ToDo attribute in text format. |
| Element | elementID | To return an element’s ID. |
| Element | documentation | To return an element’s comment/documentation. |
| Element | presentation | Element To return a presentation element. |
| Element | typeModifier | To return an element’s type modifier. |
| Element | tags | To return a list of element tags. |
| Element | text | To return a text representation of an element. In general, a 'text' property returns a string which “textually represents” the element. The string may vary depending on the symbol properties and environment options. This property returns the result from OpenAPI.RepresentationTextCreator.getRepresentedText(Element element) |
| Element | slots | To return a list of element’s slots. |
| Element | elementURL | To return an element’s URL. |
| Classifier | baseClassifier | To return a base classifier of an element. |
| BehavioredClassifier | realizedInterface | To return a Classifier’s Realized Interface. |
| Package | appliedProfile | To return a list of profiles applied to a package. |
| Stereotype | metaClass | To return a Meta Class of a stereotype. |
| DurationConstraint | min | To return a minimum value of the Duration Constraint. |
| DurationConstraint | max | To return a maximum value of the Duration Constraint. |
| TimeConstraint | min | To return a minimum value of the Time Constraint. |
| TimeConstraint | max | To return a maximum value of the Time Constraint. |
| MultiplicityElement | multiplicity | To return a multiplicity value. |
| Property | navigable | To return a navigable value. |
| Property | ownedBy | To return a property’s owner. |
| Lifeline | type | To return a lifeline type. |
| Message | event | To return a message event. |
| Message | operation | To return a message operation. |
| Message | signal | To return a message signal. |
| Message | number | To return a message number. |
| Trigger | eventType | To trigger an event type. |
| RequirementsUseCase | number | To return a use case number. |

Some predefined variables, which are not part of the UML specifications, are usable in the templates:

- **$elements** contains a list of all the elements selected from the element scope.
- **$packageScope** contains a selected package from the element scope.
- **$elementScope** contains selected elements from the element scope.
- **$empty** contains a String for empty value, which is specified from the Output Options pane.

***humanName*** and ***humanType*** are locale-specific text. If you plan to generate a report in different languages, try to avoid ***humanType*** and ***humanName***. Instead, use ***elementType*** for a non-locale-specific report.

1267789097

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec7982b4-ff4d-4abc-86eb-f91469c14184"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1267789098</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="63adc69f-9695-4d84-a477-5e39e24696de"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1267789101</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="390e76fe-223b-400d-8b03-9a9c6d6b579c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1267789100</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The variables imported to the template are collected by the type of element selected in the package scope. Use the fourth step in the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog to select the package scope. In this example, we take a class diagram with the class name Customer, see figure below, to print it in a report.</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Class Diagram: Customer"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Customer.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template variables" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Class Diagram: Customer.</h6><p>To print attributes of the Customer class in a report</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click the <strong>Customer</strong> class diagram and open the <strong>Specification</strong> dialog. The element type will appear on the dialog title, and the attribute names will appear on the right-hand side of the dialog box (see the following figure).</li></ul><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Specification Dialog" ac:alt="Specification Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="specification-dialog-of-class-customer.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template variables" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />The Specification dialog.</h6><p>With the following VTL code, you can print the attributes of the Class element:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea352c96-9589-4e17-8535-cde5f9bd9767"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($class in $Class)
   Name: $class.name
   Owner:  $class.owner.name
   Visibility: $class.visibility
   Is Abstract: $class.isAbstract
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output will be as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aa06cd57-6dba-4c7e-9e97-6c569efcbec5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Name: Customer
Owner:  com
Visibility: public
Is Abstract: false]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The table below gives and explains the additional properties which are not part of the UML specifications, but retrievable by Report Wizard.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Element Owner </th><th>Property Name </th><th>Function </th></tr><tr><td> Diagram</td><td>image</td><td>To return the diagram’s image.</td></tr><tr><td> Diagram</td><td>diagramType</td><td>To return the diagram’s type.</td></tr><tr><td> Element</td><td>image</td><td>To generate the element’s image <br />or to print an empty text if the <br />element does not refer to any <br />diagram.</td></tr><tr><td> Element</td><td>elementType</td><td><p>To return a name of an element’s type <br />(metaclass / stereotype) in lowercase <br />letters and without spaces. For example:</p><ul><li>return &quot;usecase&quot; for a Use Case</li><li>return &quot;class&quot; for a Class</li><li>return &quot;callbehavioraction&quot; for a <br />Call Behavior Action</li><li>return &quot;flowport&quot; for a Flow Port <br />(a stereotype defined in SysML v1)</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td> Element</td><td>humanName</td><td>To return an element’s human readable <br />name, which is a string concatenation <br />between the human element type and <br />the element’s name.</td></tr><tr><td> Element</td><td>humanType</td><td>To return an element’s type in a <br />human readable format.</td></tr><tr><td> Element </td><td>appliedStereotype </td><td>To return an applied stereotype <br />of an element.</td></tr><tr><td> Element </td><td>activeHyperlink </td><td>To return an active hyperlink <br />of an element.</td></tr><tr><td> Element </td><td>hyperlinks </td><td>To return all hyperlinks <br />attached to an element.</td></tr><tr><td> Element </td><td>toDo </td><td>To return an element’s <br />ToDo attribute in text format.</td></tr><tr><td> Element </td><td>elementID </td><td>To return an element’s ID.</td></tr><tr><td>Element</td><td>documentation</td><td>To return an element’s <br />comment/documentation.</td></tr><tr><td>Element </td><td>presentation</td><td>Element To return a <br />presentation element.</td></tr><tr><td>Element</td><td>typeModifier </td><td>To return an element’s <br />type modifier.</td></tr><tr><td>Element </td><td>tags</td><td>To return a list of element <br />tags.</td></tr><tr><td>Element</td><td>text </td><td><p>To return a text representation <br />of an element. In general, a 'text' <br />property returns a string which <br />“textually represents” the element. <br />The string may vary depending on <br />the symbol properties and environment <br />options. This property returns the result <br />from OpenAPI.</p><pre>RepresentationTextCreator.getRepresentedText(Element element)</pre></td></tr><tr><td>Element</td><td>slots</td><td>To return a list of element’s slots.</td></tr><tr><td>Element</td><td>elementURL</td><td>To return an element’s URL.</td></tr><tr><td>Classifier</td><td>baseClassifier</td><td>To return a base classifier of an element.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">BehavioredClassifier</td><td colspan="1">realizedInterface</td><td colspan="1">To return a Classifier’s Realized Interface.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Package</td><td colspan="1">appliedProfile</td><td colspan="1">To return a list of profiles applied to a package.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Stereotype</td><td colspan="1">metaClass</td><td colspan="1">To return a Meta Class of a stereotype.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">DurationConstraint</td><td colspan="1">min</td><td colspan="1">To return a minimum value of the Duration Constraint.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">DurationConstraint</td><td colspan="1">max</td><td colspan="1">To return a maximum value of the Duration Constraint.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">TimeConstraint</td><td colspan="1">min</td><td colspan="1">To return a minimum value of the Time Constraint.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">TimeConstraint</td><td colspan="1">max</td><td colspan="1">To return a maximum value of the Time Constraint.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">MultiplicityElement</td><td colspan="1">multiplicity</td><td colspan="1">To return a multiplicity value.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Property</td><td colspan="1">navigable</td><td colspan="1">To return a navigable value.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Property</td><td colspan="1">ownedBy</td><td colspan="1">To return a property’s owner.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Lifeline</td><td colspan="1">type </td><td colspan="1">To return a lifeline type.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Message</td><td colspan="1">event </td><td colspan="1">To return a message event.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Message</td><td colspan="1">operation</td><td colspan="1">To return a message operation.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Message</td><td colspan="1">signal</td><td colspan="1">To return a message signal.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Message </td><td colspan="1">number</td><td colspan="1">To return a message number.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Trigger </td><td colspan="1">eventType </td><td colspan="1">To trigger an event type.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">RequirementsUseCase</td><td colspan="1">number </td><td colspan="1">To return a use case number.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>Some predefined variables, which are not part of the UML specifications, are usable in the templates:</p><ul><li><em><strong>$elements</strong></em> <br />contains a list of all the elements selected from the element scope.</li><li><em><strong>$packageScope</strong> <br /></em>contains a selected package from the element scope.</li><li><em><strong>$elementScope</strong></em> <br />contains selected elements from the element scope.</li><li><em><strong>$empty</strong></em> <br />contains a String for empty value, which is specified from the Output Options pane.<br /> </li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2607a233-2fec-4462-a298-b2c654473161"><ac:rich-text-body><p><em><strong>humanName</strong></em> and <em><strong>humanType</strong></em> are locale-specific text. If you plan to generate a report in different languages, try to avoid <em><strong>humanType</strong></em> and <em><strong>humanName</strong></em>. Instead, use <em><strong>elementType</strong></em> for a non-locale-specific report.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81746c96-3fcb-4937-bbc7-d6817cd3eef5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1267789097</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="52c59f72-92ed-47d6-bd57-28f5f30ef27e" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738450 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04170: (2026x) Terminate End Event

- page_id: `272738450`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738450/2026x+Terminate+End+Event
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:45:16.067+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x) End Events
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Terminate End Event indicates that all activities in a process have to be immediately ended, including all the instances of multi-instance activities. The process will be ended without any compensation or event handling.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related element**

- End Events

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating and Using a BPMN Event' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The process will be ended without any compensation or event handling.</p><p><strong>Notation</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="end_event_terminate.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Terminate End Event" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related element</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link" data-uuid="de636edf-e57e-448e-bef0-44a01a604e9d"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) End+Events">End Events</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link" data-uuid="9c0a0cda-101f-479c-9062-b7c126eff741"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Process+Diagram">BPMN Process Diagram</a></li><li data-uuid="dc349d3b-69b3-4228-9f6b-2cfcfd9c117b"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Collaboration+Diagram">BPMN Collaboration Diagram</a></li><li data-uuid="a49cb6a9-2143-4ad5-a9b2-4547012afbf8"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Choreography+Diagram">BPMN Choreography Diagram</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedure</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="5410de66-a507-4628-8e24-6bc392fd5408"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating and Using a BPMN Event" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating and Using an Event]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737810 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04172: (2026x) Text Box

- page_id: `272737810`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737810/2026x+Text+Box
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:42:31.068+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Auxiliary diagram symbols
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A text box is a box where you can type any text. Text in the text box can be displayed as plain text or HTML text.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a text box

You can format the text box symbol properties in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Formatting symbols' space='']**Symbol Properties**dialog.

To switch the text of the text box to HTML or Plain text do one of the following

- On the diagram pane, select the text box. Click the button that appears on the lower left corner of the shape:
  - The HTML button converts the text to HTML text. [ATTACHMENT filename='switch_to_html.png']
  - The Plain button converts the text to plain text. [ATTACHMENT filename='swith_to_plain_text.png']
- Open the selected text’s shortcut menu and select or click to clear theHTML Textcommand.

You can edit the HTML text using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) HTML editor' space=''].

To add a hyperlink to an element on a text box

1. Select one or more elements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model Browser' space=''] , or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space=''] .
2. Drag the selected elements on the text box on the diagram pane. The hyperlinks to specifications of dragged elements are created on the text box. Click the link on the text box to open the Specification window of the selected element. [ATTACHMENT filename='links_in_text_box.png']

#### NOTE: Text Format

Text Format

After the hyperlink to the element on the text box is added, the text format in the text box changes to HTML.

**Related pages**

- Model elements
- Auxiliary Diagram Symbols
- Diagramming

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Click the button that appears on the lower left corner of the shape:<ul><li>The <strong>HTML</strong> button converts the text to HTML text.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="switch_to_html.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Text Box" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>The <strong>Plain</strong> button converts the text to plain text. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="swith_to_plain_text.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Text Box" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li><li><p><span>Open the selected text’s shortcut menu and select or click to clear the </span><span>HTML Text </span><span>command. </span></p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>You can edit the HTML text using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) HTML editor" /></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p><span>To add a hyperlink to an element on a text box</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Select one or more elements in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Model Browser" /></ac:link>, or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Drag the selected elements on the text box on the diagram pane. The hyperlinks to specifications of dragged elements are created on the text box. Click the link on the text box to open the Specification window of the selected element. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="links_in_text_box.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Text Box" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69c621ef-a2ce-4cda-a480-b404befa590f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Text Format</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>After the hyperlink to the element on the text box is added, the text format in the text box changes to HTML. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Model+elements">Model elements</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Auxiliary+diagram+symbols">Auxiliary Diagram Symbols</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Diagramming">Diagramming</a></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735393 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04173: (2026x) Text tool

- page_id: `272735393`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735393/2026x+Text+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:20.490+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

62959207

62959209

62959208

A Text tool provides extra functions to convert or format text such as change text to bold, italics, add an underline, equals sign etc. The following figure shows a class diagram to represent the TextTool API.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Class Diagram: TextTool.

Like other Custom tools, the Text tool (*text.jar*) must be installed in the 'extensions' folder of the Report Wizard plugin. For further information on Custom tools and installation, see *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom tool' space='']*.

To use the APIs of Texttool or other custom tools

1. Place the jar file in the extensions folder of the Report Wizard plugin folder.
2. Start the modeling tool.
3. Import it to your report by typing the following:

```text

```

62959206

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e3596ca-88a4-48db-8dbf-4ab2c399d664"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">62959207</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6253de1a-1045-434d-bfdf-7895b4162c8c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">62959209</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88653ea2-c9f6-42dc-b580-8f98b8ff2116"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">62959208</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A Text tool provides <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c9ea2912-8208-4c80-9629-e42bdb9ef18a">extra functions</ac:inline-comment-marker> to convert or format text such as change text to bold, italics, add an underline, equals sign etc. The following figure shows a class diagram to represent the TextTool API.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="A Class Diagram - TextTool.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Text tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A Class Diagram: <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9f66118b-0c2b-45e3-b514-6ec606b0f054">TextTool</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</h6><p>Like other Custom tools, the Text tool (<em>text.jar</em>) must be installed in the 'extensions' folder of the Report Wizard plugin. For further information on Custom tools and installation, see <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom tool" /></ac:link></em>.</p><p>To use the APIs of Texttool or other custom tools</p><hr /><ol><li>Place the jar file in the <strong>extensions</strong> folder of the Report Wizard plugin folder.</li><li>Start the modeling tool.</li><li>Import it to your report by typing the following:</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a07ad1d-c5b2-4c95-a508-79b937338cad"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('text', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.TextTool')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85f87ca4-3ee7-4584-a2e6-2783d2e9b333"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">62959206</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="70c17d73-1a9a-4ae5-b958-a8cb3099265a" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738898 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04174: (2026x) Text tool API

- page_id: `272738898`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738898/2026x+Text+tool+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:45.476+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Text tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Class diagram: TextTool

###### noLineBreak(String text)

This method removes all control characters such as U+0009 (Tab), U+000A (Line Feed), and U+000D (Carriage Return) from a given text. This method also filters text under the category "Cc" in the Unicode specification.

#### INFO: An example of a use case that contains multiple lines

false

An example of a use case that contains multiple lines

**Code line:**

```text
$usecase.name
```

**The output:**

Line 1

Line 2

Line 3

#### INFO: An example of the $text.noLineBreak() method

false

An example of the $text.noLineBreak() method

**Code line:**

```text
$text.noLineBreak($usecase.name)
```

**The output:**

```text
Line 1 Line 2 Line 3
```

###### toInteger(String text)

This method converts a string argument to a signed decimal integer.

#### INFO: An example of the toInteger(String text) method

false

An example of the toInteger(String text) method

**Code line:**

```text
#set ($int = $text.toInteger('2'))
```

```text
#set ($result = $int + 1)
```

```text
Result is $result
```

###### toDouble(String text)

This method converts a string argument to a signed decimal double.

****

#### INFO: An example of the toDouble(String text) method

false

An example of the toDouble(String text) method

**Code line:**

```text
#set ($d = $text.toDouble('10.1'))
```

```text
#set ($result = $d + 2)
```

```text
Result is $result
```

###### italic(String text)

This method forces a report to render a given text in the italic style.

#### INFO: An example of the italic(String text) method

false

An example of the italic(String text) method

**Code line:**

```text
The $text.italic($class.name) must provide interface for web services.
```

**The output:**

The*BusinessServices*must provide interface for web services.

###### bold(String text)

This method forces a report to render a given text in the bold style.

#### INFO: An example of the bold(String text) method

false

An example of the bold(String text) method

**Code line:**

```text
Method $text.bold($operator.name) must be implemented by implementation class.
```

**The output:**

Method**doService**must be implemented by implementation class.

###### underline(String text)

This method forces a report to render a given text in the underlined style.

#### INFO: An example of the underline(String text) method

false

An example of the underline(String text) method

**Code line:**

```text
Attribute $text.underline ($attribute.name) contains a service name.
```

**The output:**

AttributeserverNamecontains a service name.

###### html(String text)

This method forces a report to render a given text as HTML.

#### INFO: An example of the html(String text) method

false

An example of the html(String text) method

**Code line:**

```text
$text.html('<ul><li>A</li><li>B</li></ul>')
```

**The output:**

- A
- B

For more information on supported *HTML* tags, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Appendix D: HTML tag support' space=''].

****

###### getString(String text)

This methods converts text from *RTF* to a Java String.

#### INFO: An example of the getString(String text) method

false

An example of the getString(String text) method

**Code line:**

```text
#set ($str = $text.getString("Ubersetzer"))
```

**The output:**

Ubersetzer

###### equals(String str1, String str2)

This method compares two strings. The result will be true if and only if str1 represents the same sequence of characters as str2 does. This method supports *RTF* text comparison.

#### INFO: An example of the equals(String str1, String str2) method

false

An example of the equals(String str1, String str2) method

**Code line:**

```text
#set ($str = $text.getString("Ubersetzer"))
```

```text
#if ($text.equals($str, "Ubersetzer"))
```

```text
true
```

```text
#end
```

###### getPureText(String HTMLString)

This method gets plain text from an HTML documentation. All HTML tags that are applied to the text will be removed from the text. Only plain text would be returned.

#### INFO: An example of the getPureText(String HTMLString) method

false

An example of the getPureText(String HTMLString) method

**Code line:**

```text
#set ($html = '<html><body><b>Text</b></body></html>')
	$text.getPureText($html)
```

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729398 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04175: (2026x) The supported HTML lists

- page_id: `272729398`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729398/2026x+The+supported+HTML+lists
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:35:03.313+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Appendix E: DocBook Support
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The DocBook engine of Report Wizard supports the following HTML lists:

1. Unordered Lists
2. Ordered Lists
3. Definition Lists

#### Unordered Lists

An unordered list is defined by the <ul> element. An unordered list element <ul> contains one or more <li> elements that define the actual items of the list in bullets. The Unordered List element shown in the first figure below will be rendered in a DocBook format as in the second figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Ordered Lists

An ordered list is defined by the <ol> element. The ordered list element <ol> contains one or more <li> elements that define the actual items of the list preceded by numbers. The unordered list element shown in the first figure below will be rendered in a DocBook format as in the second figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Definition Lists

A definition list is defined by <dl> element. A definition list element <dl> consists of two parts: (i) A term that is created using the <dt> element and (ii) a description that is created using the <dd> element. The definition list supports one to multiple terms and the corresponding definition(s). The term comes before the definition. 
The definition list element shown in the first figure below will be rendered in a DocBook format as in the second figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

An unordered list element &lt;ul&gt; contains one or more &lt;li&gt; elements that define the actual items of the list in bullets. The Unordered List element shown in the first figure below will be rendered in a DocBook format as in the second figure below.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="HTML Tag for a Fragment of Unordered List" ac:alt="HTML Tag for a Fragment of Unordered List"><ri:attachment ri:filename="HTML tag for fragments of unordered list.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) The supported HTML lists" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="DocBook Tag for a Fragment of Unordered List" ac:alt="DocBook Tag for a Fragment of Unordered List"><ri:attachment ri:filename="DocBook tag for fragments of unordered list.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) The supported HTML lists" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>Ordered Lists</h2><p>An ordered list is defined by the &lt;ol&gt; element. The ordered list element &lt;ol&gt; contains one or more &lt;li&gt; elements that define the actual items of the list preceded by numbers. The unordered list element shown in the first figure below will be rendered in a DocBook format as in the second figure below.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="HTML Tag for a Fragment of Ordered List" ac:alt="HTML Tag for a Fragment of Ordered List"><ri:attachment ri:filename="HTML tag for fragments of ordered list.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) The supported HTML lists" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="DocBook Tag for a Fragment of Ordered List" ac:alt="DocBook Tag for a Fragment of Ordered List"><ri:attachment ri:filename="DocBook tag for fragments of ordered list.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) The supported HTML lists" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>Definition Lists</h2><p>A definition list is defined by &lt;dl&gt; element.
