<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735159 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04190: (2026x) Traceability

- page_id: `272735159`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735159/2026x+Traceability
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:57.566+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Model Sample

525815827

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Model Sample

525815831

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Model Sample

525815830

#### INFO: Compatibility

Compatibility

The traceability solution is available in Architect and Enterprise editions.

Our modeling tools support the traceability feature that allows you to track, visualize, navigate, and analyze the elements involved in the traceability relations. The traceability relations are used to relate the elements representing the same concept in different UML models at different levels of abstraction or from different viewpoints. Different levels of abstraction may contain, include, or even correspond to different stages of system development process (starting from requirements analysis and finishing with implementation). The higher level of abstraction (e.g., requirements analysis), contains models with specifying elements, and the lower level of abstraction (e.g., implementation) includes models with realizing ones.

The traceability relations help to determine how your requirements or the other model artifacts are satisfied. As they may change, you can use traceability relations to monitor the impact of these changes.

Multiple types of custom and extended UML relationships (e.g., realization, abstraction), tags (e.g., **Alternative Flow of Events Diagrams** ), and properties (e.g., **Owned Behavior** ) are used to represent traceability relations between the specifying and realizing elements for showing traceability from requirements analysis to implementation and deployment models.

**Relation Map and Dependency Matrix**

You can visualize the traceability relations of your project in order to analyze them using the modeling tool's features, such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Relation Map' space=''] (for the analysis of traces among multiple levels of abstraction) and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Dependency Matrix' space=''] (for the analysis of traces between any two levels of abstraction).

**Element’s Specification window, Properties panel, Go To, and Notes**

You may track and navigate the elements, that are directly or indirectly related to a specific element through the traceability properties that will be represented in a special property group of the element’s [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space='']and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Traceability properties in Properties panel' space=''], or on the element’s shortcut menu under the **Go To** menu. The traceability properties can also be visualized on a diagram using the standard modeling tool mechanism for displaying property values in notes.

**Traceability Report**

A Traceability Report is particularly useful when there is a need to visualize and verify that requirement analysis, design, and implementation model elements are all covered in higher or lower levels of abstraction, for example, all use cases should be covered with design classes realizing them.

#### INFO: Model Sample

Model Sample

The model used in all the examples in this section is the **Traceability** sample model that comes with our modeling tool. To open this sample do one of the following:

- On the Welcome screen, select Samples > Product Features > Traceability .
- Go to the folder <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\product features and open the Traceability.mdzip file.

525815826

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="92bf424b-1621-4e69-8a56-dc631d7179d1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">525815827</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c1817e6-8acb-4736-9ca9-0c837ca27455"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">525815831</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4266576-3d03-4701-a87d-b961cf27410d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">525815830</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><br /></p><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8f59b00d-4a98-40a7-a839-0ba09ab5bc64"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Compatibility</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The traceability solution is available in Architect and Enterprise editions.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Our modeling tools support the traceability feature that allows you to track, visualize, navigate, and analyze the elements involved in the traceability relations. The traceability relations are used to relate the elements representing the same concept in different UML models at different levels of abstraction or from different viewpoints. Different levels of abstraction may contain, include, or even correspond to different stages of system development process (starting from requirements analysis and finishing with implementation). The higher level of abstraction (e.g., requirements analysis), contains models with specifying elements, and the lower level of abstraction (e.g., implementation) includes models with realizing ones. </p><p><span>The traceability relations help to determine how your requirements or the other model artifacts are satisfied. As they may change, you can use traceability relations to monitor the impact of these changes. </span></p><p><span>Multiple types of custom and extended UML relationships (e.g., realization, abstraction), tags (e.g., </span> <strong>Alternative Flow of Events Diagrams</strong> <span>), and properties (e.g., </span> <strong>Owned Behavior</strong> <span>) are used to represent traceability relations between the specifying and realizing elements for showing traceability from requirements analysis to implementation and deployment models. </span></p><p><strong>Relation Map and Dependency Matrix </strong></p><p><span>You can visualize the traceability relations of your project in order to analyze them using the modeling tool's features, such as <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Relation Map" /></ac:link> (for the analysis of traces among multiple levels of abstraction) and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dependency Matrix" /></ac:link> (for the analysis of traces between any two levels of abstraction). </span></p><p><strong>Element’s Specification window, Properties panel, Go To, and Notes </strong></p><p><span>You may track and navigate the elements, that are directly or indirectly related to a specific element through the traceability properties that will be represented in a special property group of the element’s <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification window" /></ac:link> </span>and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Traceability properties in Properties panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Properties panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or on the element’s shortcut menu under the <strong>Go To</strong> menu. The traceability properties can also be visualized on a diagram using the standard modeling tool mechanism for displaying property values in notes.</p><p><strong>Traceability Report</strong></p><p>A Traceability Report is particularly useful when there is a need to visualize and verify that requirement analysis, design, and implementation model elements are all covered in higher or lower levels of abstraction, for example, all use cases should be covered with design classes realizing them.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35369f24-024f-43e8-8ac7-189c34275092"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Model Sample</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="472ddac2-ca79-4b5b-8fd4-b3376fcab4e6">The model used in all the examples in this section is the <strong>Traceability</strong> sample model that comes with our modeling tool. To open this sample do one of the following: </ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ul><li>On the Welcome screen, select <strong>Samples &gt; Product Features &gt; Traceability</strong>. </li><li>Go to the folder <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\product features </em>and open the<em> Traceability.mdzip</em> file. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p></div></div><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bf3a7bcb-977a-4679-80ed-9573ae8702be"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">525815826</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="6e08ea25-04cf-402e-8f19-ee8353fec6c2" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735215 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04191: (2026x) Traceability properties in Go To submenu

- page_id: `272735215`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735215/2026x+Traceability+properties+in+Go+To+submenu
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:04.934+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Traceability > (2026x) Traceability relations representation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The submenu of the Go To menu on the element’s shortcut menu allows you to easily find and navigate to therelated elements through the traceability relations.

To select an element from the higher/lower level of abstraction in the Containment tree

1. Select the element in the Containment tree or its shape on the diagram.
2. From the element’s shortcut menu, select Go To > Traceability > Specification/ Realization/ Other , choose a property, and then select an element.
3. The element will be selected in the Containment tree.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Traceability properties in the Go To submenu

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<div class="column"><p><span>The submenu of the </span> <span>Go To </span> <span>menu on the element’s shortcut menu allows you to easily find and navigate to the </span>related elements through the traceability relations.</p><p><span>To select an element from the higher/lower level of abstraction in the Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Select the element in the Containment tree or its shape on the diagram.</li><li><span>From the element’s shortcut menu, select </span> <span>Go To </span> <span>&gt; </span> <span>Traceability </span> <span>&gt; </span> <span>Specification/ Realization/ Other</span><span>, choose a property, and then select an element. </span></li><li><span>The element will be selected in the Containment tree. </span> </li></ol></div><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="traceability_properties_goto_menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Traceability properties in Go To submenu" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="440e93af-1b6b-44bb-ac54-f3301b3d5346">Traceability properties in the Go To submenu</ac:inline-comment-marker></h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735207 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04192: (2026x) Traceability properties in notes

- page_id: `272735207`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735207/2026x+Traceability+properties+in+notes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:03.724+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Traceability > (2026x) Traceability relations representation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The traceability properties can be visualized on a diagram using the standard modeling tool mechanism displaying the property values in notes.

To visualize an element’s traceability with other elements in the note on a diagram

1. Create a note for the element, whose traceability properties you want to visualize.
2. From the note’s shortcut menu, select Edit Compartments > Element Properties .
3. In the list on the left-hand side, click the traceability property of you choice (e.g., Realizing Class or Specifying Use Case) and then click the > button.
4. Click OK. Now you can view the element’s traceability with other elements. [ATTACHMENT filename='traceability_properties_in_notes.png'] Traceability properties in the notes on a diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>The traceability properties can be visualized on a diagram using the standard modeling tool mechanism displaying the property values in notes. </span></p><p><span>To visualize an element’s traceability with other elements in the note on a diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Create a note for the element, whose traceability properties you want to visualize.</li><li><span>From the note’s shortcut menu, select </span> <span>Edit Compartments </span> <span>&gt; </span> <span>Element Properties</span> <span>.</span></li><li><span>In the list on the left-hand side, click the traceability property of you choice (e.g., </span> <span>Realizing </span>Class or Specifying Use Case) and then click the &gt; button.</li><li><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>Click </span> <span>OK</span><span>. Now you can view the element’s traceability with other elements. </span></p></div></div> <ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="traceability_properties_in_notes.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Traceability properties in notes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><div class="layoutArea"><h6 class="column" style="text-align: center;"> Traceability properties in the notes on a diagram </h6></div></li></ol></div></div>
````

## PAGE 04193: (2026x) Traceability properties in Properties panel

- page_id: `272735211`

- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Traceability** tab in the element’s **Properties** panel (at the bottom of the Browser window) is one of the places, wherein the element’s traceability properties, showing its realizing and/or more specific elements, are represented.

To view an element’s traceability properties in its **Properties** panel

1. Select the element in the Containment tree or its shape on the diagram.
2. In the Model Browser, click on the Properties panel > Traceability tab. Now you can view element’s traceability properties. [IMAGE alt='' src='']Traceability tab in the Properties panel

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735190 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04194: (2026x) Traceability properties in Specification window

- page_id: `272735190`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735190/2026x+Traceability+properties+in+Specification+window
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:01.523+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Traceability > (2026x) Traceability relations representation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Traceability property group in element’s Specification window is one of the places, wherein the element’s traceability properties, showing its realizing and/or more specific elements, are represented.

To view an element’s traceability properties in its Specification window

1. Select an element and open the element’s Specification window by right clicking on the element.
2. Click the Traceability property group.
3. Now you can view element’s traceability properties. [IMAGE alt='' src='']Traceability property group in the Specification window

The following picture gives an example of the traceability relations between the elements from different abstraction levels of the same project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of single level traceability relations

As you see, the “Create User” use case from the “Requirements” package is covered by two design classes, and these classes are accordingly specified by this particular use case.

Therefore, the traceability properties such as **Realizing Class and Realizing Element**represent both design classes as realizing elements of this use case. Accordingly, the traceability properties such as **Specifying Use Case**of each design class represent the “Create User” use case as a more specific element.

Note that the same related element can be represented through different traceability properties.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Realizing and Specifying traceability properties in Specification window

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><div class="column"><p><span>The Traceability property group in element’s Specification window is one of the places, wherein the element’s traceability properties, showing its realizing and/or more specific elements, are represented. </span></p></div></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>To view an element’s traceability properties in its Specification window</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element and open the element’s Specification window by right clicking on the element.</li><li>Click the Traceability property group. </li><li>Now you can view element’s traceability properties. <h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="specification_of_use_case_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Traceability properties in Specification window" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);"><span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);">Traceability property group in the Specification window</span> </span></h6></li></ol><p>The following picture gives an example of the traceability relations between the elements from different abstraction levels of the same project. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="single_level_traceability.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Traceability properties in Specification window" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"> An example of single level traceability relations </h6><p>As you see, the “Create User” use case from the “Requirements” package is covered by two design classes, and these classes are accordingly specified by this particular use case. </p><p style="text-align: left;">Therefore, the traceability properties such as <strong>Realizing Class and Realizing Element </strong>represent both design classes as realizing elements of this use case. Accordingly, the traceability properties such as <strong>Specifying Use Case </strong>of each design class represent the “Create User” use case as a more specific element.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: left;">Note that the same related element can be represented through different traceability properties. </p><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column" style="text-align: center;"><div class="layoutArea"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specification_of_class_loginDialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Traceability properties in Specification window" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><h6><span> Realizing and Specifying traceability properties in Specification window </span></h6></div></div></div></div></div></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735422 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04195: (2026x) Traceability properties in Traceability Report

- page_id: `272735422`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735422/2026x+Traceability+properties+in+Traceability+Report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:22.424+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Traceability > (2026x) Traceability relations representation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Traceability Report feature supports the coverage analysis and publishes elements that are related to the selected elements through the traceability relations. You can generate a report either for the whole project or for a selected part of the project. This feature is the output of the coverage analysis.

Coverage analysis provides the visibility of each element’s related artifacts, indicated as realizing (lower level of abstraction) and/ or specifying (higher level of abstraction) ones.

The main objective of the Traceability Report is to visualize and verify that Analysis, Design, and Implementation model elements are all covered.

The Traceability Report provides the ability to:

- Find the areas of uncovered parts.
- According to the report information, create additional artifacts to increase coverage.
- Measure the coverage quantitatively.
- Identify the redundant artifacts.

To generate a traceability report of the selected scope

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard **.**
2. In the Select Template area, select Traceability > Traceability and then click Next .
3. Click Next .
4. In the **Select Element Scope** area, define the scope of the report by using the buttons placed between two lists, and then click Next .
5. In the Output Options area, define the appropriate options.
6. Click **Generate**. Your traceability report will be generated (generation time depends on the selected scope). [IMAGE alt='' src='']A fragment of Traceability Report

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>The Traceability Report feature supports the coverage analysis and publishes elements that are related to the selected elements through the traceability relations. You can generate a report either for the whole project or for a selected part of the project. This feature is the output of the coverage analysis. </span></p><p>Coverage analysis provides the visibility of each element’s related artifacts, indicated as realizing (lower level of abstraction) and/ or specifying (higher level of abstraction) ones.</p><p>The main objective of the Traceability Report is to visualize and verify that Analysis, Design, and Implementation model elements are all covered.</p><p>The Traceability Report provides the ability to:</p><ul><li>Find the areas of uncovered parts.</li><li>According to the report information, create additional artifacts to increase coverage. </li><li>Measure the coverage quantitatively.</li><li>Identify the redundant artifacts. </li></ul><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span> <br /> </span></p><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>To generate a traceability report of the selected scope</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span>On the </span> <strong>Tools </strong> <span>menu, click </span> <strong>Report Wizard</strong> <span> <strong>.</strong> </span></li><li><span>In the </span> <strong>Select Template </strong> <span>area, select </span> <strong>Traceability &gt; Traceability </strong> <span>and then click </span> <strong>Next</strong> <span>.</span></li><li><span>Click </span> <strong>Next</strong> <span>.</span></li><li><span>In the </span> <span> <strong>Select Element Scope</strong> </span> <span>area, define the scope of the report by using the buttons placed </span>between two lists, and then click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Output Options </strong>area, define the appropriate options.</li><li><p>Click <strong>Generate</strong>. Your traceability report will be generated (generation time depends on the selected scope). </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="traceability_report.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Traceability properties in Traceability Report" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);"> A fragment of Traceability Report </span></h6></li></ol></div></div></div></div></div></div>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736157 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04196: (2026x) Traceability relation maps

- page_id: `272736157`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736157/2026x+Traceability+relation+maps
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:47.374+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Managing traceability and impact analysis
- labels: ['traceability-relation-maps', 'capability-structural-map', 'capability-behavioral-map', 'implementation-map', 'operational-activity-implementation-map', 'operational-activity-map', 'organization-structure-map', 'system-resource-map', 'variant-map', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Relation Map diagram enables you to review and analyze relations rapidly between the elements of the whole model.

The UAF Plugin presents you a number of predefined relation maps. You will be able to change the context and scope.

You can change the context by dragging the selected element onto the map.

The predefined relation maps are the following:

##### Capability Behavioral Map

The **Capability Behavioral Map** depicts the behavioral relationships of a Capability. This predefined map includes the Capability itself, related Operational Activities (Operational Activity, Standard Operational Activity), related Functions (Function, Project Activity, Security Process), and related Service Functions. The Capability Behavioral Map also shows the internal decomposition of each mapped behavioral element, as well as the relationships among these elements.

##### Capability Structural Map

The **Capability Structural Map** depicts the structural relationships of a Capability. This predefined map includes the Capability itself, related Operational Agents (Operational Performer, Operational Architecture), Resource Performers (Capability Configuration, Organization, Software, and other elements), Services, Actual Resource, and Actual Service. The Capability Structural Map also shows the internal compositions of each mapped element, as well as the relationships among these elements.

##### Implementation Map

The **Implementation Map** depicts the implementation relationships of an Operational Performer. This predefined map includes the Operational Element (Operational Performer, Operational Architecture, Operational Interface, Operational Role, Operational Information), and the Resource Elements (Resource Performer, Resource Role, Resource Interface, Resource Information) that have been indicated as implementing that operational element. The Implementation Map also shows the internal compositions of each mapped element, as well as the relationships among these elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Operational Activity Decomposition Map

The **Operational Activity Decomposition Map** depicts the behavioral decomposition of an Operational Activity.

##### Operational Activity Implementation Map

The **Operational Activity Implementation Map** depicts the behavioral implementation of an Operational Activity, relating the Operational Activity and Functions that implement the operational behavior. When the Operational Activity has composite behaviors and Functions have composite functionality, the Operational Activity Implementation Map also includes the behavioral elements for the composed elements.

##### Operational Performer Impact Analysis Map

The **Operational Performer Impact Analysis Map** depicts the model elements influenced by the changes of the Operational Performer. This predefined map includes:

- Performs Operational Activity
- Exhibits Capability
- Context (Operational Roles, where Operational Performer is used as Context)
- Inputs and Outputs (Operational Performers connected using Operational Exchanges)
- Operational Exchanges

##### Operational Role Impact Analysis Map

The **Operational Role Impact Analysis Map**depicts the model elements influenced by the changes of the Operational Role. This predefined map includes:

- Context (Operational Performer that is the context of the Operational Role)
- Inputs and Outputs (Operational Roles connected using Operational Connectors)
- Performs In Context (Operational Activities performed by the Operational Role)
- Performs (by Type) (Operational Activities performed by the Type of the Operational Role)
- Exhibits Capability (by Type) (Capabilities exhibited by the Type of the Operational Role)

##### Organization Structure Map

The **Organization Structure Map** depicts the structural composition of an Actual Organization. The predefined map includes:

- Actual Organization itself
- Actual Posts
- Actual Persons

The Organization Structure Map illustrates the relationships between these organizational elements.

##### Resource Performer Map

The **Resource Performer Map** depicts the structural decomposition of a Resource Performer including Capability Configuration, Organization, Software, and other elements.

##### Resource Role Impact Analysis Map

The **Resource Role Impact Analysis Map** depicts the model elements influenced by the changes of the Resource Role. This predefined map includes:

- Context (Resource which is used as the context of the Resource Role)
- Inputs and Outputs (Resource Roles connected using Resource Connectors)
- Performs In Context (Functions performed by the Resource Role)
- Performs (by Type) (Function Actions performed by the Type of the Resource Role)
- Implements (by Type) (Operational Performers implemented by the Type of the Resource Role)
- Exhibits Capability (by Type) (Capabilities exhibited by the Type of the Resource Role)

##### Strategic Driver Map

**The Strategic Driver Map (St-Mv)**depicts the relationships of a Driver.

This predefined map includes the Driver, Enterprise Goals, and/or Enterprise Objectives that are related to a Driver using the Motivated By relationship, and Actual Strategic Phases that are related to the Enterprise Goals or Enterprise Objectives using the Phases relationship.

The Context of the relation map can be either a Driver or a Package. The Relationship Criteria include Owned Element (if the Context Element is Package), Motivated By, Phases, Is Composed of Enterprise Goals, and Is Composed of Enterprise Objectives. The Element Type includes Driver, Enterprise Goal, Enterprise Objective, and Actual Strategic Phase.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Variant Map

The **Variant Map** depicts the specializations of a Resource Performer, including Capability Configuration, Software, and other elements. Specializations of a Resource Performer are modeled using the Generalization relationship.

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Relation Map diagram enables you to review and analyze relations rapidly between the elements of the whole model.</p><p>The UAF Plugin presents you a number of predefined relation maps. You will be able to change the context and scope.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a815edba-6ddc-4108-9f70-02174da37c82"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can change the context by dragging the selected element onto the map.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="confluence-link">The predefined relation maps are the following:</p><p class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0983454-d2b0-4d94-bfa5-ca17ef10aa9c" /></p><h3>Capability Behavioral Map</h3><p>The <strong>Capability Behavioral Map</strong> depicts the behavioral relationships of a Capability. This predefined map includes the Capability itself, related Operational Activities (Operational Activity, Standard Operational Activity), related Functions (Function, Project Activity, Security Process), and related Service Functions. The Capability Behavioral Map also shows the internal decomposition of each mapped behavioral element, as well as the relationships among these elements. </p><h3>Capability Structural Map</h3><p>The <strong>Capability Structural Map</strong> depicts the structural relationships of a Capability. This predefined map includes the Capability itself, related Operational Agents (Operational Performer, Operational Architecture), Resource Performers (Capability Configuration, Organization, Software, and other elements), Services, Actual Resource, and Actual Service. The Capability Structural Map also shows the internal compositions of each mapped element, as well as the relationships among these elements. </p><h3>Implementation Map</h3><p>The <strong>Implementation Map</strong> depicts the implementation relationships of an Operational Performer. This predefined map includes the Operational Element (Operational Performer, Operational Architecture, Operational Interface, Operational Role, Operational Information), and the Resource Elements (Resource Performer, Resource Role, Resource Interface, Resource Information) that have been indicated as implementing that operational element. The Implementation Map also shows the internal compositions of each mapped element, as well as the relationships among these elements. </p><p><ac:image ac:title="Creating Implementation Map" ac:alt="Creating Implementation Map"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Implementation_map.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Traceability relation maps" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Operational Activity Decomposition Map</h3><p>The <strong>Operational Activity Decomposition Map</strong> depicts the behavioral decomposition of an Operational Activity. </p><h3>Operational Activity Implementation Map</h3><p>The <strong>Operational Activity Implementation Map</strong> depicts the behavioral implementation of an Operational Activity, relating the Operational Activity and Functions that implement the operational behavior. When the Operational Activity has composite behaviors and Functions have composite functionality, the Operational Activity Implementation Map also includes the behavioral elements for the composed elements. </p><h3>Operational Performer Impact Analysis Map</h3><p>The <strong>Operational Performer Impact Analysis Map</strong> depicts the model elements influenced by the changes of the Operational Performer. This predefined map includes:</p><ul><li>Performs Operational Activity</li><li>Exhibits Capability</li><li>Context (Operational Roles, where Operational Performer is used as Context)</li><li>Inputs and Outputs (Operational Performers connected using Operational Exchanges)</li><li>Operational Exchanges</li></ul><h3>Operational Role Impact Analysis Map        </h3><p>The <strong>Operational Role Impact Analysis Map </strong>depicts the model elements influenced by the changes of the Operational Role. This predefined map includes:</p><ul><li>Context (Operational Performer that is the context of the Operational Role)  </li><li>Inputs and Outputs (Operational Roles connected using Operational Connectors)</li><li>Performs In Context (Operational Activities performed by the Operational Role)</li><li>Performs (by Type) (Operational Activities performed by the Type of the Operational Role)</li><li>Exhibits Capability (by Type) (Capabilities exhibited by the Type of the Operational Role)</li></ul><h3>Organization Structure Map</h3><p>The <strong>Organization Structure Map</strong> depicts the structural composition of an Actual Organization. The predefined map includes:</p><ul><li>Actual Organization itself</li><li>Actual Posts</li><li>Actual Persons</li></ul><p>The Organization Structure Map illustrates the relationships between these organizational elements.</p><h3>Resource Performer Map</h3><p>The <strong>Resource Performer Map</strong> depicts the structural decomposition of a Resource Performer including Capability Configuration, Organization, Software, and other elements.</p><h3>Resource Role Impact Analysis Map</h3><p>The <strong>Resource Role Impact Analysis Map</strong> depicts the model elements influenced by the changes of the Resource Role. This predefined map includes:</p><ul><li>Context (Resource which is used as the context of the Resource Role)</li><li>Inputs and Outputs (Resource Roles connected using Resource Connectors)</li><li>Performs In Context (Functions performed by the Resource Role)</li><li>Performs (by Type) (Function Actions performed by the Type of the Resource Role)</li><li>Implements (by Type) (Operational Performers implemented by the Type of the Resource Role)</li><li>Exhibits Capability (by Type) (Capabilities exhibited by the Type of the Resource Role) </li></ul><h3>Strategic Driver Map</h3><p><strong>The Strategic Driver Map (St-Mv) </strong>depicts the relationships of a Driver.</p><p>This predefined map includes the Driver, Enterprise Goals, and/or Enterprise Objectives that are related to a Driver using the Motivated By relationship, and Actual Strategic Phases that are related to the Enterprise Goals or Enterprise Objectives using the Phases relationship.   </p><p>The Context of the relation map can be either a Driver or a Package. The Relationship Criteria include Owned Element (if the Context Element is Package), Motivated By, Phases, Is Composed of Enterprise Goals, and Is Composed of Enterprise Objectives. The Element Type includes Driver, Enterprise Goal, Enterprise Objective, and Actual Strategic Phase.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_driver_map.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Traceability relation maps" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Variant Map</h3><p>The <strong>Variant Map</strong> depicts the specializations of a Resource Performer, including Capability Configuration, Software, and other elements. Specializations of a Resource Performer are modeled using the Generalization relationship. </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="3e61ded7-6246-4140-b283-6f70bc0cef81" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735188 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04197: (2026x) Traceability relations representation

- page_id: `272735188`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735188/2026x+Traceability+relations+representation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:43:07.730+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Traceability
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The traceability relations are represented through the so-called traceability properties that have been predefined for each element type according to the traceability method supported in the modeling tool.

A single traceability property shows an element or a set of elements that are related to a particular element through the relationships which are specified by some traceability rule. The predefined traceability rules are stored in the Traceability customization profile used by the MagicDraw Profile.

The properties for the traceability relations are grouped into the realization, specification, and other groups, owning both direct (single level) and indirect (multiple levels) traceability relations. The indirect traceability relations are represented by the so-called **All**properties, for example, **All Specifying Elements**, **All Realizing Elements**, and **All Specific Classifiers**.

The relations for traceability [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating traceability relations' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Navigating between different levels of abstraction' space=''] is also possible without using traceability properties. However, the customizable model driven traceability properties, which represent the traceability relations in a single place, can greatly help in traceability information visualization and access.

#### INFO: Tip

Tip

You can customize the predefined traceability properties according to your needs or create your own traceability properties and specify your own rules.You can also customize the grouping of the traceability properties according to your needs. For more information, please, refer to the sections [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties' space='UPDG'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Property Groups and Subgroups' space='UPDG'].

Element’s traceability properties are represented in the following places:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Traceability properties in Specification window' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Traceability properties in Properties panel' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Traceability properties in notes' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Traceability properties in Go To submenu' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Traceability properties in Traceability Report' space='']

**Related pages**

- Traceability
  - Creating traceability relations
  - Traceability relations representation
  - Navigating between different levels of abstraction
  - Analyzing traceability relations
  - Predefined traceability rules
  - Custom traceability rules

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729964 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04198: (2026x) Tracing between data model layers

- page_id: `272729964`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729964/2026x+Tracing+between+data+model+layers
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:36:18.976+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Data-related modeling > (2026x) Transformations > (2026x) ER to SQL (generic / Oracle) transformations
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After the transformation, a relationship is established between the logical data model layer, which is represented by the ER model, and the physical data model layer, which is represented by a SQL model respectively. It is possible to navigate between the connected elements in the forward (ER -> SQL) and backward (SQL -> ER) directions using the dedicated submenu - **Go To**- on the element’s shortcut menu.

To go to the corresponding element in the forward direction

1. Right-click the element.
2. On it’s shortcut menu, click Go To > Traceability > Model Transformations > Transformed T o .

To go to the corresponding element in the backward direction

1. Right-click the element.
2. On it’s shortcut menu, click Go To > Traceability > Model Transformations > Transformed From .

The same tracing information is visible in the element’s Specification window and **Properties**panel under the **Traceability**tab. This information is also reflected in the Entity-Relationship and SQL Report using navigable references between the report section. Traceability information can also be depicted in a relation map or in a tabular format using the capabilities of the custom dependency matrix feature.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>After the transformation, a relationship is established between the logical data model layer, which is represented by the ER model, and the physical data model layer, which is represented by a SQL model respectively. It is possible to navigate between the connected elements in the forward (ER -&gt; SQL) and backward (SQL -&gt; ER) directions using the dedicated submenu - <strong>Go To </strong>- on the element’s shortcut menu.</p><p>To go to the corresponding element in the forward direction</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the element.</li><li>On it’s shortcut menu, click <strong>Go To </strong>&gt; <strong>Traceability </strong>&gt; <strong>Model Transformations </strong>&gt; <strong>Transformed T</strong><strong>o</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To go to the corresponding element in the backward direction</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the element.</li><li style="text-align: left;">On it’s shortcut menu, click <strong>Go To </strong>&gt; <strong>Traceability </strong>&gt; <strong>Model Transformations </strong>&gt; <strong>Transformed From</strong>.</li></ol><p>The same tracing information is visible in the element’s Specification window and <strong>Properties </strong>panel under the <strong>Traceability </strong>tab. This information is also reflected in the Entity-Relationship and SQL Report using navigable references between the report section. Traceability information can also be depicted in a relation map or in a tabular format using the capabilities of the custom dependency matrix feature.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731036 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04199: (2026x) Tracing requirements

- page_id: `272731036`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731036/2026x+Tracing+requirements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:00.184+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Requirements management > (2026x) Analyzing Requirements
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

831113593

831113595

831113591

**On this page**

43

831113594

The traceability solution is available in Architect and Enterprise editions.

The traceability relations help determine how your requirements or other model artifacts are satisfied. As they change, you can use traceability relations to monitor the impact of these changes. You can visualize traceability relations using MagicDraw features, such as the Dependency Matrix or Relation Map. Requirements tracing is a background for analysis and is concerned with recovering the sources of requirements and predicting the effects of requirements. Tracing is fundamental to performing impact analysis when requirements change. A requirement should be traceable backwards to the criteria and stakeholders which motivated it (from functional requirements to business requirements). A requirement should also be traceable forwards into the design entities that satisfy it.

Traceability makes it easier for you to:

- Find, navigate and define the realization and specification of elements from different levels of abstraction:
- Understand why an element exists, how the element is realized, and clarify the purpose of the element.
- Validate the system functionality (to see if it meets customer requirements and if any superfluous functionality has been implemented).
- Improve a customer's understanding of the system and thus his/her acceptance of the system.
- Have single places for relations analysis used in traceability.
- Check artifact coverage and completeness.
- Bridge the gap between levels of abstraction.
- Trace elements by using out-of-the-box predefined traceability suites.

For detailed instructions on how to use the traceability solution, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Traceability' space=''].

##### Traceability relations in Specification window

In the selected element's Specification window, you can see traceability relations as properties. You can also create your own specific properties and edit them here. 
 
 To trace element relations in the Specification window

1. Open the Specification window of the selected element.
2. Scroll through the general requirement's properties to get to the Traceability properties list.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Traceability property group in element's specification window

For more information about using a Specification window, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space=''].

##### Traceability relations in Properties panel

The same traceability properties are displayed in a Properties panel. 
 
 To trace element relations in the Properties panel

1. Select the element in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model Browser' space=''] or the shape on the diagram pane.
2. In the Model Browser, click the Properties panel > Element tab and scroll through the properties to get to the Traceability properties list.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Traceability property group in element's properties panel.

For more information about using the Properties panel, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Properties tab' space=''].

##### Representing traceability on Notes

To trace element relations in a Note

1. Select the element's shape on the diagram pane.
2. Connect a note to the selected shape.
3. Right-click the note and click Edit Compartments on the shortcut menu,or select a note and click the Edit Element Properties button appearing on the right side of the note. [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_element_properties.png']
4. In the Compartment Edit dialog, click the Element Properties tab, select the traceability properties you want to represent on a note and move them from the Hidden to Selected list.
5. Click OK when you are done. Selected traceability properties are presented on a note.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Traceability properties on a note

##### Tracing relations using Go To command

The Go To command allows you to easily find and navigate to the related elements through the traceability relations. 
 
To trace element relations using the Go To command

1. Select the Requirement in the Containment tree or the shape on the diagram pane.
2. Right-click the selected Requirement. On the shortcut menu, click Go To > Traceability > Specification / Realization / Owner / Other and select the desired element. This element is selected in the Containment tree.

For more information about traceability, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Traceability' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737279 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04200: (2026x) Tracking elements affected by Requirement changes

- page_id: `272737279`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737279/2026x+Tracking+elements+affected+by+Requirement+changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:48:26.551+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Suspect Links
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To enable tracking

1. In the main menu, go to Analyze > Suspect Links and select Enable Tracking .
2. In the **Enable Tracking**message dialog
  - choose to track Entire Model , or
  - click Specific Scope to choose packages, where the relationships, Requirements, and the design elements you want to track are.
3. Click **OK**. If you're working on a TWCloud project, commit it for the changes to apply.

To change scope

1. Go to Analyze > Suspect Links > Tracking Scope .
2. In the **Select Package** dialog, choose packages, where the relationships, Requirements, and the design elements you want to track are, then click **OK**. If you're working on a TWCloud project, commit it for the changes to apply.

To check for suspicions

1. Modify the linked Requirements as per your needs.
2. If you're working on a TWCloud project, commit it for the changes to apply.
3. In the main menu, go to Analyze > Suspect Links , and select Show Suspicions .

The detected suspect links are provided in the **Validation Results** panel.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To handle suspicions

* marked steps are only available in the TWCloud project.

1. Suspected model elements are highlighted in yellow and marked with a small triangle symbol. [ATTACHMENT filename='suspect_links_marked_yellow.png']
2. To see changes in a Requirement, right-click the validated cell and select **Show Changes***. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Review Requirement changes*. [ATTACHMENT filename='requirement_changes.png']
4. Update design elements according to changes in the Requirement.
5. Clear that suspicion. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Solving untracked links issue

If you notice that some links in your model are not tracked for some reason, you can start tracking them manually.

To start tracking links in your model

1. Right-click the untracked link (it is marked with an exclamation mark in a yellow triangle).
2. Select the Start Tracking solver in the opened menu. [ATTACHMENT filename='nottrackedlink.png']

**Description**

A Transaction is a specialized type of SubProcess whose special behavior is controlled through a transaction protocol (such as WS-Transaction).
